title: 14-Page Franchise Prospectus Design
desc: I’m looking for a designer-writer team that can turn our agriculture franchise story into a polished, 14-page prospectus aimed squarely at attracting new franchisees. We already have a full set of branding guidelines—logo usage, colour palette, fonts, image style—so every spread should stay on brand while still feeling fresh and impactful. Scope of work • Structure and layout the complete 14-page document, balancing copy, imagery and white space so it reads smoothly both on-screen and in print. • Craft or refine persuasive copy that highlights our market opportunity, support systems, training programme and projected returns—everything a potential franchisee needs to say “yes”. • Develop clear infographics and tables for key numbers and timelines. • Supply final files as press-ready PDF plus an editable source file (InDesign or Illustrator preferred). Acceptance criteria • Design adheres 100 % to the supplied brand guidelines. • All text is error-free, investor-friendly and targeted to prospects with no prior agriculture background. • Visuals remain crisp when printed at A4 and when viewed on tablets. • Delivery of both final and editable files within the agreed timeframe. I’ll provide the raw content outline, brand assets and high-resolution photos the moment we kick off. Let me know your estimated turnaround time and any portfolio pieces that show similar franchise or investment brochures you’ve completed.
mcp_workflow_v1: search_design (multi-page prospectus / investment brochure template) -> asset_add_file (logo, hi-res photos) -> fill_text (market opportunity, support, training, projected returns across 14 pages) -> asset_preview_file -> document_convert_pdf (A4 press PDF)

title: Convert AI Images to Print-Ready Vectors for Large-Format Trade Show Display
budget: Fixed price Entry Level Est. budget: $50.00
desc: We are seeking an experienced graphic designer and Adobe Illustrator expert to recreate AI-generated raster images into clean, highly optimized vector artwork. These vectors will be printed on a large-format Graffiti Backlit Trade Show Display for an upcoming software convention. Because of the size and the backlit material, the files must be perfectly engineered for print—standard "auto-tracing" will not work. Key Responsibilities: Vector Tracing: Manually redraw or intelligently trace the provided AI images to create scalable vectors. Node Optimization: Aggressively manage and simplify anchor points to ensure clean paths. We cannot accept files with millions of nodes that will crash printing RIP software. Print Prep: Set the document color profile to CMYK and adjust the palette so the colors print accurately on a backlit fabric display. Template Formatting: Apply the finalized vector art to our manufacturer's exact print template, ensuring all safe zones and bleed lines are strictly followed. Required Deliverables: Fully editable source files (.AI). Production-ready, high-resolution PDF for professional printing. Scalable EPS and SVG formats. Ideal Candidate Profile: Must have verifiable experience preparing files for professional printing and large-format design (trade show booths, banners, or billboards). Expert-level proficiency in Adobe Illustrator. Strong understanding of vectorizing complex, multi-colored AI-generated art without losing the original stylistic intent. To Apply: Please provide examples of previous large-format print designs you have completed. Specifically mention your process for cleaning up AI-generated raster images.

Deliverables • Two separate, print-ready files: 10 x 10 ft and 10 x 15 ft • High-resolution mock-ups from at least two viewpoints • All fonts converted to outlines and colours set in CMYK I’ll supply the logo files once we start. Let me know if you also need exact fabric templates from my manufacturer or additional brand elements, and please share a couple of canopy or large-format designs you’ve done so I can see your style aligns with mine.
mcp_workflow_v1: search_design (Express large-format canopy/banner template) -> fill_text (brand name/tagline) -> asset_add_file (client logo) -> image_vectorize -> document_render_vector (CMYK print-ready vector per size)

title: Format 20-Page Illustrated Kindle
desc: I have written a 20-page book and gathered the artwork, but right now everything lives in a chain of emails—each message holds its slice of text and the matching illustration. I need those loose pieces turned into a single, polished Kindle file that Amazon will accept without complaint. Here is what you will work with • Twenty sets of text + high-resolution images delivered via email threads (I will forward or share in a folder). • A simple page-by-page outline so you know exactly which picture pairs with which text. What I expect at hand-off • A fully formatted, illustration-rich file ready for Kindle Direct Publishing (EPUB or KPF—whichever you prefer to achieve the best result). • Consistent page breaks, margins, and image sizing so the layout looks great on Fire tablets as well as the Kindle app. • A quick screen-capture or Kindle Previewer report that proves the file passes Amazon’s checks. Logistics • Turnaround: one week from the day you receive all assets. • Fixed fee: the price we agree on upfront is final; please bid only if that works for you. I’m happy to answer technical questions fast so you can stay on schedule. Looking forward to seeing the book come to life.

title: Modernized InDesign Catalogue Creation
desc: 1. Project Overview • Project: Design of product catalogue and template pages. • Format: InDesign files, with final version as multi-page printed A4 booklet and downloadable PDF. • Core Objective: To create a clean, professional, and easy-to-navigate trade catalogue, maintaining brand continuity with previous years while modernising and simplifying the layout. The catalogue will be built out in Adobe InDesign with multiple reusable and interchangeable page designs. 2. Brand & Style Guidelines • Reference Style: We would like to modernise and simplify the current Massey Harpers Game and Country catalogue. • Visual Language: It’s a catalogue for farmers and gamekeepers. The design needs to be in keeping for that audience, easy to read, simple, clean and professional. We would like to avoid giving an impression of expensive or corporate. • Consistency: Every page must include: o Header: Dedicated space for the section title and page numbers. o Footer: Clear contact information in the footer of each page, with space for disclaimer and/or pricing information, e.g. “prices exclude VAT”. 3. Page Structure & Layout Front Cover • Imagery: Full-page "hero" image with Massey Harpers company logo. • Text: Catalogue name prominently at the top; the Year positioned at the bottom with secondary headline. Back Cover • Imagery: Full-page image with Massey Harpers company logo. • Contact Information: Company contact information: o Phone number o Email address o Website URL o Physical address Informational/Introduction Page • Contents: Clear list of contents with page numbers • About: Company bio/introduction • Contact: Detailed contact information • Legal: Space for terms and conditions Feed Page Design To closely follow the existing feed guide design (page 3): • Space for two/three/four logos at the top • Feed guide in current table format • Include “feed experts” contact information with the following: o Image of expert o Contact information: name, phone number email address and job title • Notes or disclaimer information Product/Inside Page Designs We would like at least five different page designs that we can use to build out the catalogue. These pages will be used throughout the catalogue and include multiple products in a simple grid layout, but may differ by including the following: • Banner advertisements/images/callouts (see HF seeds banner on page 20 of current catalogue) • Notice or disclaimer spaces (see safety notice on page 21 of current catalogue) • Highlighted/special offer products • Product range sections (see automatic drinker spaces on page 16) • Full-page and half-page advertisements Product Information • Required Elements per Product: o Product image (either white background or lifestyle) o Product name o Product description o Price (with option for quantity, e.g. per 100 and price on application) o Product code/SKU o QR code (for linking the product on the website) 4. Technical Requirements • Software: Final files to be delivered in Adobe InDesign format (.indd) with a print-ready PDF export. • Assets: o Reference 1: Massey Harpers Game and Country 2026 PDF o Reference 2: Collins Nets Game & Poultry Catalogue 2026 PDF 5. Timeline & Delivery • Draft 1 (Concept/Master Pages): [Insert Date] • Draft 2 (Full Layout): [Insert Date] • Final Amendments: [Insert Date] • Complete Version: [Insert Date] ________________________________________ Notes 1. Typography: Since the Massey Harpers site uses a mix of traditional and clean sans-serif fonts, please pick a font that remains legible even in small product descriptions.

title: Redesign of Product Brochures
desc: Brochure Design & Brand Template Creation for Engineering / Industrial Company We are looking for an experienced graphic designer to redesign and modernise a suite of brochures for Polymax, one of the UK’s leading manufacturers and distributors of rubber solutions and engineering products. The project forms part of a wider rebrand alongside the launch of a new contemporary website, so the brochure designs must align closely with the new digital brand style. Scope of Work The project includes: Redesign of existing brochures Creation of a new corporate brochure Design of multiple new product family brochures Creation of editable brochure templates for future internal use Development of a consistent visual style system across all brochures Creation of a set of custom icons for use in both brochures and on the website One brochure family (O-rings) will require: A marketing-focused version A more technical version with expanded engineering information These must be visually differentiated while remaining within the same overall brand system. Design Requirements The brochures should: Reflect the styling of the new website Be modern, technical, professional and visually engaging Appeal primarily to engineers, technical buyers and industrial distributors Use strong imagery with concise copy Incorporate icons, tables, bullet points and clean layouts Be adaptable for use by a sister company (alternative colour variants and logos) The initial brochure format will be: A5 Print-ready and digital PDF formats Deliverables The successful freelancer will provide: Print-ready PDF artwork Adobe InDesign packaged files with bleed setup Editable InDesign templates SVG and PNG icon assets created during the project Assets Provided We will supply: Existing brochures Draft brochure content Product information Brand guidance Images and graphics Icon requirements list Working Style This will be a collaborative and iterative project with several rounds of feedback. The Marketing Manager will act as the main point of contact throughout the project. Ideal Freelancer We are looking for someone with: Strong brochure/editorial design experience Experience creating corporate or industrial marketing materials Excellent typography and layout skills Ability to create clean technical visuals/icons Strong Adobe InDesign skills Attention to detail and consistency across multiple documents Experience working with engineering, manufacturing or industrial brands would be highly beneficial. Timeline Target completion dates: Corporate, O-Rings & Gaskets brochures: 26 June 2026 AV Mounts & Mouldings brochures: 17 July 2026 Site Safety, Fenders, Silicone & Technical O-Ring brochures: 31 July 2026

title: Lightroom Classic Expert for High-End Product Photo Masking, Color Balancing & Alignment (45 Images)
budget: Fixed price Expert Est. budget: $250.00
desc: We are looking for a meticulous Lightroom Classic expert to assist with a product photography project for an upscale e-commerce stationery brand. The project consists of 45 commercial listing images shot tethered in Lightroom Classic. The images feature various sized, gray suede-covered boxes on a gray suede background, using blank dummy cards as placeholders. Because of lighting and exposure shifts during the shoot, we need the background and boxes balanced perfectly across all images to match a provided "Hero" sample shot. Furthermore, some of the boxes shifted slightly during the shoot and need to be digitally straightened. - Crucial Project Requirements: Instead of destructive editing or standard global adjustments, you will be utilizing Lightroom Classic's native tools so that we can retain full editability for the future. For every image, you will need to complete two main phases: PHASE 1: GEOMETRY & REALIGNMENT * Use Lightroom’s Transform panel (Guided Upright, Rotate, Aspect, etc.) to perfectly straighten the boxes, fix any minor lens distortion, and ensure lines are square and professional across all 45 frames. PHASE 2: ADVANCED PRECISE MASKING Create three distinct, highly accurate local adjustment masks. **CRITICAL NOTE: Some shots include styling props (e.g., a simple paperweight, champagne glass, or plate). All three masks must precisely exclude/go around these objects so that the props themselves remain completely untouched by any adjustments. 1. Mask 1 (The Background): The flat surface suede and the sweep of fabric in the background (excluding all props). 2. Mask 2 (Box Fronts): The front-facing, shadowed/mid-tone planes of the boxes (excluding any overlapping props). 3. Mask 3 (Box Tops & Highlights): The top surfaces of the boxes and any highlighted side edges (excluding any overlapping props). Once the masks are created, you will use them to balance the exposure, contrast, and tones of each photo to match our master sample shot. **Important Note on Quality: Your masking must be incredibly precise. We will later be using these files in Photoshop to overlay colors onto the background for our digital mockups. The masks must seamlessly hug the boxes and clean edges around the props so that the background can accept both delicate pastel colors and rich, dark colors while maintaining a realistic suede texture and natural transitions. (Note: You do not need to worry about masking the blank dummy cards/envelopes themselves, as these will be entirely replaced later). Deliverables: * A Lightroom Catalog (.lrcat) or Smart Previews/XMP sidecar files containing the 45 edited images with the three requested masks intact and geometry corrected for every photo. * Perfectly leveled, straightened, and color-balanced images that match the exposure and tone of the provided sample photo. Project Type & Hiring Process: * Project Scale: Medium (45 images). * Trial Project: We will begin with a paid test milestone of 2 images to evaluate your precision, attention to detail, and geometry alignment. Upon approval, we will fund the remaining balance for the rest of the batch. Qualifications / Skills Needed: * Expert-level proficiency with Lightroom Classic (specifically advanced masking tools, precise brush/object subtraction for complex prop boundaries, and the Transform/Lens Corrections panels). * A sharp eye for commercial product photography alignment, exposure matching, and color theory. * Experience prepping files for high-end e-commerce, mockups, or digital compositing. * Strong communication skills and ability to follow precise technical guidelines. To Apply: Please share examples of your previous product editing, geometry correction, or masking work. Please include the phrase "Suede Perfection" at the top of your proposal so we know you have read the entire description.

desc: I need a professional academic methodological framework diagram, clean flat vector infographic style, isolated on a pure white background. The layout is structured vertically from top to bottom into three distinct, color-coded sections. Top section is labeled 'K12 Schooling': It shows a horizontal flowchart starting with a square node labeled 'Asks Question in Class', pointing to 'Shame-Inducing Response'. This node splits into two parallel paths: a short-term path labeled 'Learns Help-Seeking is Risky' and a long-term path labeled 'Impacts Mathematical Identity'. Middle section is labeled 'Workforce Training': It begins with a square node labeled 'Re-engaging with Mathematics Learning'. An arrow connects this to a circular cyclic loop containing two nodes with two-way arrows between them: 'Having Challenges' and 'Embodied Mathematics Identity'. An exit arrow from this loop points to a terminal node labeled 'Help-Avoidance'. A prominent, dashed diagonal arrow labeled 'Justification Pathway' connects the top 'Impacts Mathematical Identity' node directly down to the middle 'Embodied Mathematics Identity' node. Bottom section is labeled 'Research Methodology': A large bracket gathers data from the top two sections and points down into a funnel icon labeled 'Narrative Data Collection'. An arrow points from the funnel into a hexagon labeled 'Weiner's Attribution Theory', and a final arrow exits to a box labeled 'Research Insights'. Clean minimalist design, simple geometric shapes, sharp thin lines, highly professional textbook style, no 3D elements, no realistic textures."

title: Instructional / Training Content Developer Build Course Modules (Content + Activities + Handouts)
desc: We are developing an 8 Module training program for organizational transformation on a technical topic (sustainability) We need an experienced instructional designer with strong visual design and PowerPoint skills to develop the full learning materials for each module, including the core training content, learner activities, and supporting handouts. Work will be delivered module by module so we can review and approve each one before moving to the next. This is a content development AND visual design role. We need someone who can take learning objectives and (often complex or technical) source material and turn it into clear, visually engaging, adult-learning-friendly material with polished, professional PowerPoint decks at the core, supported by other design tools where they elevate the result. Scope of Work 1. Training Content (PowerPoint-led) - Structured lesson content mapped to clear learning objectives - A polished, visually engaging PowerPoint deck(facilitator- and/or self-paced-ready) - Custom visuals - diagrams, infographics, process flows, data visualizations that simplify complex or technical concepts - Logical flow with intros, key concepts, examples, and summaries 2. Learner Activities - 2 activities per module (e.g. discussions, scenarios, case studies, quizzes, role-plays, group exercises) - Clear instructions, timing, and intended outcomes - Where relevant, gamified or interactive elements 3. Handouts & Aids - Participant worksheets, reference sheets, checklists, or templates - Clean, branded, print- and screen-ready formatting 4. Assessment / Knowledge Checks - Short quizzes or reflection prompts to confirm understanding We will provide source material / outlines / brand guidelines edit as applicable. You will be responsible for the instructional design and final polished materials. Working Approach (Module-by-Module) - We work in milestones — one module per milestone - For each module: draft → our review → revisions → approval → next module. - Each module should be self-contained but consistent in style, tone, and template with the others. - We expect [1–2] rounds of revisions per module included. - Deliverable Formats - Editable PowerPoint (PPTX) source files for all decks - Editable handout files (DOCX/PDF) where appropriate - Source/export files for any custom graphics created in other tools - All files using a consistent template/branding we agree on in Module 1 Required Skills & Experience - Advanced PowerPoint skills — you can build clean, modern, visually compelling decks (not bullet-heavy text dumps); strong command of layouts, animation, master slides, and templates - Visual design ability — comfortable using additional tools (e.g. Canva, Adobe Illustrator/Photoshop, Figma, infographic or diagramming tools) to create custom graphics, icons, and data visualizations - Proven experience developing training material for organizational transformation OR highly technical topics — you can take dense, complex, or change-heavy content and make it clear and engaging (please share relevant samples) - Proven instructional design / curriculum development experience - Strong understanding of adult learning principles (ADDIE, Bloom's, Kirkpatrick, or similar) - Excellent writing and content-structuring skills in English - Ability to design engaging activities and assessments — not just slides - Reliable, communicative, and able to work to milestone deadlines Nice to have: e-learning authoring tools (Articulate Storyline/Rise, Captivate), motion/animation skills, and experience translating technical SME input into learner-friendly content. How to Apply Please include in your proposal: 1. A short intro and why this project fits your experience. (NO AI PLEASE) 2. 2–3 samples of PowerPoint training materials you've developed ideally for organizational transformation or technical topics that show your visual design quality. 3. Your approach to developing one module (high-level process). 4. Your rate (per module) and estimated turnaround per module. We're looking for a long-term collaborator for the full build, so a strong first module could lead to all remaining modules.

title: Masterclass Video Editing Specialist
budget: Hourly: $5.00 - $20.00 Intermediate Est. time: Less than 1 month, Less than 30 h
desc: Overview We're a Nordic C-suite peer-learning company and have recorded a ~90-minute CFO masterclass: a short host intro, two CFOs walking through their financial setups via screen share (≈30 min each), and a ~30-min audience Q&A. We need it edited into a polished, engaging video that feels alive — not a static meeting capture. This is the first in a recurring series, so we're looking for someone we can keep working with. Footage you'll receive Screen recording (Full HD) — the presenters' screens; this is the primary visual during the two walkthroughs. 1–2 camera angles (Full HD, horizontal 16:9, 25/30fps) — presenter and room. Multitrack audio (Nomono): one clean WAV per speaker + a room/ambient track. Use the per-speaker tracks as the primary audio. A Google Meet recording — as a sync reference and backup only. Scope & editing direction Sync all camera angles + the screen recording to the audio (a hand-clap marks the start of each segment). Cut to a rhythm that keeps ~90 minutes feeling lively — natural cuts between angles, never static. When a presenter refers to something on screen, show the screen share. During Q&A, cut to the room / the person asking. Light audio balancing (the Nomono tracks are already AI-enhanced; level voices as needed). I am comfortable with whichever tool you prefer—Adobe Premiere Pro, Final Cut, DaVinci Resolve, CapCut, etc.—as long as the output is crisp, properly formatted (1080 × 1920), and instantly publishable. Acceptance criteria • All clips match the original audio levels and color tone; no abrupt cuts or dead air. • Text overlays remain legible on mobile screens. • No copyrighted audio or visuals are introduced. Share a recent sample of similar spiritual or educational shorts you have produced, note your typical turnaround time per batch, and let me know any suggestions you have for maximizing engagement. I’m ready to start as soon as I find the right editor.

title: Video Editor + Motion Graphics Creator — American Football Educational Course (30 Videos)
desc: ⚠️ READ THIS ENTIRE POST BEFORE APPLYING. THERE IS A REQUIRED WORD IN YOUR APPLICATION (see below). ABOUT THE PROJECT I’m the creator of an online course that teaches people how to understand, watch, and love American football. The course covers everything from basic rules to offensive and defensive formations, play types, player positions, game strategy, and more. The course has approximately 30 video lessons. Each lesson features me on camera, presenting the material directly to students. My job is done — the on-camera footage is recorded and the script is delivered. Your job is to transform that raw footage into a polished, engaging lesson video. WHAT YOU’LL DO Each video will require one or both of the following: 1. Slide Integration — I will provide pre-made slides. You will cut to the slide at the right moment in my script, hold it on screen as I explain the concept, and then cut back to me on camera. Clean, professional timing. 2. Motion Graphics / Animation / Explainer Overlay — Some concepts require motion to understand. For example: a receiver running a route, a defensive line shifting, a play developing in real time. You will create motion graphics, animated diagrams, or short explainer animations that visually demonstrate the concept while my voice plays over it. NON-NEGOTIABLE: YOU MUST UNDERSTAND AMERICAN FOOTBALL This is a hard requirement, not a preference. You cannot create accurate motion graphics for a play-action pass, a cover-2 defense, or a pick route if you don’t know what those things are. We are not in a position to teach you the game while you’re building the videos. You must be able to: • Name and explain basic offensive formations (e.g., I-formation, shotgun, spread) • Understand what “down and distance” means and how game flow works • Animate a receiver route accurately (go route, slant, out, curl, etc.) • Identify player positions on both sides of the ball • Recognize the difference between offensive and defensive schemes at a conceptual level If you’re not sure whether you qualify — you probably don’t. Please do not apply. WHAT I PROVIDE • My on-camera lesson video (cleaned, trimmed, and properly lit) • Full written script with timestamps for where slides and graphics appear • Pre-designed slide assets (PowerPoint or similar) • Brand colors, fonts, and style guide (after test phase) • Clear notes on what each graphic needs to show SKILLS REQUIRED • Video editing (Premiere Pro, Final Cut, or DaVinci Resolve) • Motion graphics and animation (After Effects strongly preferred) • Sports animation experience (any sport is a plus; American football is ideal) • Experience with educational or explainer video format • Strong communication and ability to meet deadlines consistently PROJECT SCOPE This project will begin with a paid test phase of 2 videos. If the quality, communication, and workflow are excellent, this becomes a 30-video ongoing project. • Total project: approximately 30 videos • Turnaround expectation: 1-2 business days per video once assets are delivered for the initial test phase. Total project will need to be completed in 2 - 4 weeks. • Revisions: 2 rounds included per video HOW TO APPLY To be considered, your application must include ALL of the following: 1. The word “Touchdown” at the very start of your cover letter. Applications without this word will not be read. 2. 2–3 portfolio links that are relevant — motion graphics, sports animation, or educational video examples. 3. Answer to this question: Name 3 offensive formations used in American football and briefly explain when a team would use each one. 4. Your software stack (editing + motion graphics). Creating AI videos that look realistic are fantastic, as long as they are accurate. Some concepts may work for realistic AI and some may need to be animation. 5. Your realistic turnaround time per video. IDEAL CANDIDATE You’re the right person for this project if you: • Actually watch football and get excited by the idea of making it visual and accessible • Have created sports-related or educational motion graphics before • Are detail-oriented and take pride in clean, polished final products • Communicate proactively and meet deadlines without being chased • Are looking for an ongoing project — not just a one-off gig This is a meaningful project. Students across the country use this course to finally understand a game that connects them to their families, friends, and communities. The videos need to be excellent. If that excites you, I’d love to hear from you.

title: 2026 -promotional stickers
budget: Hourly Intermediate Est. time: Less than 1 month, Less than 30 hrs/week
desc: SMK HELMETS 2026 Promotional Sticker — Design Brief For: External Designer 1. Project Overview Project Annual Promotional Sticker — 2025 Edition Brand SMK Helmets (Studds Accessories Ltd.) Purpose Rider giveaway / brand promotion item, distributed with helmet purchases Print type Reflective sticker (as per previous years) Dimensions Approx. 55 mm wide × 80 mm tall (portrait). Final to be confirmed by printer. Deliverable Print-ready vector artwork (AI / EPS / PDF), CMYK, with bleed 2. Brand & Style Reference SMK Helmets produces an annual sticker each year with a consistent visual language. Two previous editions have been provided as reference: Year Theme / Title Key Elements 2023 FREEDOM Rider from behind, helmet raised, red bike, dark bg, 40×80 mm 2024 Since 2015 / Desert Skull Rider Skeleton rider front-on, desert/cactus bg, dark moody palette, 60×81 mm Consistent visual DNA across all editions: Near-black / very dark background (not pure black — dark navy or dark brown works too) Hand-drawn / tattoo-flash / graphic novel illustration style One dominant focal scene or character (no busy collages) Red as the primary accent colour (#CC0000 or similar) White for typography and highlights Minimal text used as framing, not information Portrait orientation, roughly 40–60 mm wide × 80 mm tall SMK Helmets logo / wordmark present but not dominant 3. This Year’s Concept — 2026 Theme: Off-Road / Dirt Bike The 2026 sticker moves into motocross / enduro territory — raw, adrenaline-driven, and aspirational. The scene captures a dirt bike mid-air off a jump, silhouetted against a dramatic sunset. Tagline “OWN THE DIRT” Placed at the top of the sticker, spaced/tracked out. Red rule lines on either side of the text (as seen in reference concept). Open to alternative wording if designer has suggestions. Scene Description Dirt bike is mid-air, angled nose-up — classic jump pose, viewed from a slightly low front-angle Rider is in full MX gear: helmet with peak visor, jersey, gloves, boots — standing on footpegs in attack position Sunset / backlight source directly behind the bike: deep orange-red sun creating a strong silhouette and halo effect Distant hill/mountain silhouettes in the background, layered for depth Dirt / dust spray kicking off behind the rear wheel on the jump takeoff Ground below is a dirt mound / jump lip — earthy brown tones Sky is dark with faint stars or dusk atmosphere — “golden hour into night” feel Illustration Style Hand-drawn graphic illustration — similar to tattoo flash or screen print aesthetic Strong outlines, limited colour palette, no photorealism Gritty and bold — not cartoonish or playful Refer to the 2024 skeleton rider sticker for the tone and finish quality expected 4. Colour Palette Role Colour / Hex Usage Background #0E0B04 (near-black) Base sticker background Accent (brand) #CC0000 (SMK red) Tagline, logo, bike plastics/tank, visor peak, rule lines Sun / glow #FF5500 → #FFAA00 Sunset backlight, halo around bike, horizon glow Dirt / earth #5C3A10 → #2A1A06 Ground, jump mound, dust spray White / highlight #FFFFFF Typography (SMK wordmark, year), rim highlights, stars 5. Typography Tagline “OWN THE DIRT”: Bold condensed sans-serif, tracked out, all caps — e.g. Arial Black, Impact, or similar. White fill. Positioned top-centre. Brand name “SMK”: Large, bold, white. Bottom-centre. “HELMETS”: Smaller, tracked out, light grey, below SMK wordmark. Year “2 0 2 6”: Tracked out, italic or regular, red. Bottom-most text element. Designer may propose a custom lettering style for the tagline if it better suits the illustration. 6. Technical Specifications File format: AI (preferred) or EPS / PDF print-ready Colour mode: CMYK Resolution: 300 dpi minimum if any raster elements used Bleed: 2 mm all around Print substrate: Reflective vinyl sticker material — account for reflective base in colour choices (lighter colours will glow; very dark areas may lose detail) Sticker shape: Rectangular with rounded corners (radius approx. 3–4 mm) — consistent with previous editions Dimensions: Approx. 55 × 80 mm finished size. Printer to confirm final cut size. 7. Reference Files to be Provided STICKER_2023_2024.pdf — Both previous sticker designs with dimensions annotated Concept_SVG_2025 — Digital concept sketch showing scene layout, colour palette, and composition (provided separately) The concept sketch is a direction reference only — the designer should use it as a compositional guide, not a template to trace. 9. Contact & Approvals Point of contact Shilpa — SMK Helmets / Studds Accessories Ltd. Approval required 1 round of revisions expected. Final sign-off by Shilpa before sending to printer. Notes Please share a WIP (work-in-progress) at sketch/rough stage before proceeding to final render. SMK Helmets — Ride More. Fear Less.

title: Ecom Video Editor for Meta & TikTok Ads
budget: Fixed price Intermediate Est. budget: $4,000.00
desc: About Us: We are a dynamic and rapidly growing 7-figure women's jewelry brand powered by Shopify. We are passionate about great design that speaks for itself, drawing inspiration from everyday fashion and influencers, and genuinely caring about the products we create and the relationships we build. Our marketing is performance-driven, and high-converting video creative is central to our scaling strategy. The Role: We're seeking a skilled and agile E-commerce Response Video Editor (full time / 40 hours per week) who understands how to create video ads that don't just look good but convert. You will work in lockstep with our Creative Strategist, taking raw footage (UGC, potentially studio shots) and briefs to produce engaging, platform-optimized video ads primarily for Meta (Facebook/Instagram) and TikTok. Your job is to execute rapidly, iterate effectively based on performance insights (provided by the strategist), and ensure the final output is engineered for maximum engagement and conversions. Forget long-form content; this is about short, punchy, direct-response video. IMPORTANT: This is not a role for someone juggling multiple jobs, working for or running a freelancing agency, or managing several clients at once. We are looking for a dedicated full-time video editor who will work exclusively with us and be fully committed to our brand, team, and creative output. Key Responsibilities: • Edit High-Performing Video Ads: Transform raw footage and strategist briefs into compelling video ads optimized for Meta and TikTok feeds/stories/reels. • Rapid Iteration: Quickly execute multiple variations of winning ad concepts provided by the Creative Strategist (testing different hooks, visuals, text overlays, CTAs, music). Understand that iteration based on data is key. • Platform Optimization: Expertly edit for platform best practices – strong hooks within the first 3 seconds, fast pacing, engaging captions/text overlays, appropriate aspect ratios (9:16, 1:1, 4:5), safe zones, and effective sound design. • Integrate Copy & CTAs: Seamlessly weave in primary text concepts, headlines, and clear calls-to-action as guided by the strategist. • Collaborate Closely: Maintain clear, fast communication (Slack, etc.) with the Creative Strategist to receive feedback, discuss edits, and ensure alignment with performance goals. • Asset Management: Organize and manage video files and project assets efficiently. • Meet Deadlines: Deliver high-quality edits reliably and on schedule in a fast-paced environment. Who We're Looking For: • Proven Social Ad Editing Experience: Minimum 2+ experience specifically editing short-form video advertisements for performance marketing campaigns on Meta & TikTok. A portfolio showcasing direct-response style video ads is required. • Technical Proficiency: Highly skilled in video editing software (e.g., Adobe Premiere Pro, Final Cut Pro, CapCut is a major plus). Proficiency in basic motion graphics (e.g., After Effects for text overlays) is desirable. • Platform Native: Deep understanding of what works visually on TikTok and Meta feeds/stories/reels – you know the trends, the hooks, the pacing, the sounds that grab attention immediately. • Performance-Oriented Mindset: You understand the goal is conversion, not just aesthetics. You are receptive to feedback driven by ad metrics and can adapt edits accordingly. (Links to Strategist's "Data Mastery," "Creativity That Converts"). • Speed and Agility: Ability to work quickly, execute variations efficiently, and adapt to changing requirements without sacrificing quality. (Links to Strategist's "Adaptability & Agility"). • Collaborative Spirit: Excellent communication skills and ability to work effectively and receive direction from a Creative Strategist. (Links to Strategist's "Collaboration is Key," "Clear Communication"). • Strong Work Ethic & Accountability: Reliable, proactive, meets deadlines, and takes ownership of edit quality – no babysitting needed. (Links to Strategist's "Work Ethic"). • Detail-Oriented: Ensures brand consistency, correct aspect ratios, and error-free text/graphics. Nothing sloppy makes the cut. • Basic understanding of direct response marketing principles. • Experience editing UGC content. Compensation: $2,000 monthly base salary, with the opportunity to earn monthly performance-based bonuses tied to creative contributions. Total monthly compensation can reach up to $4,000 based on performance. How to Apply: In your proposal, you MUST include (give as much detail as possible): 1. Provide your Resume/CV and link to your portfolio showcasing ECOM VIDEO ADS for Meta/TikTok.Other type of ads will not be looked at or considered. 2. Briefly describe your process for editing a typical 15-45 sec. video ad. 3. How do you handle feedback and rapid iteration requests from creative strategists? 4. List your primary editing software and mention your experience with CapCut. 5. What AI tools or software have you used to create video ads? Provide as much detail as possible and share any previous AI work if any.

title: Ecommerce marketing specialist needed — jewelry brand, Shopify, Meta ads
budget: Fixed price Expert Est. budget: $5,000.00
desc: Hi, I'm Jacques, founder of Vara Studio — we sell accessible gold jewelry with natural and lab-grown gemstones. Think everyday pieces, not occasion-only. Our Shopify store has been live for a while but honestly the sales aren't where they should be. We've put some budget into Meta and Google in the past with pretty disappointing results and I'm trying to figure out if the problem was execution, strategy, or something else entirely. I'm not looking for someone to just run ads. I want to talk to someone who can look at what we have, tell me what's broken, and propose a realistic plan to actually get this moving. Budget for ads is in the $1,000–1,500/month range. Before I invite anyone to interview I'd like you to answer a few questions below — just so I understand how you work before we spend time on a call. 1. Have you worked with a jewelry or accessories brand on Shopify before? Tell me about the brand — what was the situation when you came in and what did you actually do? 2. We've spent around $1,000/month on Meta before and got very little back. What are the most common reasons that happens, in your experience? 3. If you were starting fresh with a Shopify jewelry store and a $1,200/month ad budget — no purchase history in the pixel, maybe 200 visitors/month organic — what would your first 60 days look like? Be specific, I'm not looking for a general answer. 4. Do you handle creative or is that on us? If you need video or lifestyle content, how do you usually solve that for clients at this budget level? 5. What does a normal working month with you look like — how do we communicate, what do I get from you and how often? 6. What's your monthly fee for this kind of engagement and what does it include? If you work on a percentage of ad spend instead, tell me how that works. Please answer the questions in your proposal — I won't be moving forward with anyone who skips them. Looking to make a decision within the next week or so.

title: Graphic Designer for Social Media Ads

Design multiple variations of the same ad concept for testing hooks, layouts, text, colors, and formats. Experience with fashion, jewelry, beauty, or luxury-inspired brands. Work Details Part-time or freelance position. Remote role. Ongoing work available for the right person. title: Jewellery retouch
budget: Fixed price Intermediate Est. budget: $2,000.00
desc: Job Description: We're looking for a highly skilled jewelry retoucher (individual or team) for a long-term, ongoing collaboration — not a one-time job. Scope of Work Ongoing yearly project, not a single task. Up to 5,000 pieces per year. Each piece is photographed from multiple angles, so total volume may reach 10,000 images per year. Required Deliverables JPG files PNG files (transparent background) Quality Standards The final result must match the quality level of the attached sample images — clean metal, sharp and well-defined stones, accurate reflections, consistent lighting, clean background, and stable quality across the entire volume. Consistency across thousands of images matters just as much as the quality of a single image. What to Include in Your Proposal 1-Price per image, and whether you offer special bulk pricing for large volumes. 2-Expected turnaround time — per image or per batch — and how many images you can deliver per day. 3-Links to previous work specifically in jewelry retouching (not general retouching). 4-How you ensure consistent quality across large volumes. Selection Criteria: Final selection will be based on the best balance of highest quality and lowest price. Please make sure your sample work reflects the quality level shown in our reference images, and that your pricing is competitive for high-volume work. We prefer working with someone who has proven experience in jewelry retouching and can commit to a steady monthly volume throughout the year.
mcp_workflow_v1: asset_add_file (the jewelry shots, multiple angles per piece) -> image_remove_background (transparent PNG, clean background) -> image_apply_auto_tone + image_adjust_brightness_and_contrast + image_adjust_highlights (clean, consistent lighting across the volume) -> image_crop_and_resize (consistent framing) -> asset_preview_file -> export JPG + transparent PNG

title: Luxury Brand Identity Suite
desc: I’m building a unified luxury presence that spans graphic design, videography, photography, and an elegant website. The entire look must feel premium yet minimal: black backgrounds, crisp white type, and a typography-driven logo reminiscent of the world’s top fashion houses. I do not want icons, flourishes, or complex graphics—clean lines and impeccable spacing will let the brand speak for itself. High-quality visuals sit at the heart of this project. I’ll supply creative direction, but I need you to capture and prepare high-resolution images suitable for both retina-ready web use and large-format print. Every pixel should look flawless after scaling, so expert post-processing in Photoshop or Lightroom is essential. Once the photographic assets are secured, they will flow into social graphics, video intros/outros, and, most critically, a sleek, modern website. The site must carry the same black-and-white elegance, load quickly, and display perfectly on any screen size. Figma (or a comparable UI tool) for mock-ups and a standards-based front-end build will keep the hand-off smooth. Deliverables • Custom, typography-only logo in vector format • Brand style guide: fonts, colour specs (black/white), usage rules • A curated set of retouched, high-resolution photos (web + print) • 2–3 short video assets that match the visual tone • Website design files and responsive HTML/CSS/JS build (or WordPress theme) mirroring the brand guidelines Acceptance Criteria 1. Logo remains perfectly legible at 24 px and at billboard size. 2. All imagery exports at 300 dpi for print and 2× resolution for web without visible artefacts. 3. Website passes Google Lighthouse performance scores of 90+ on mobile and desktop. 4. Visual style across logo, photos, video, and site is consistent with the black-and-white luxury aesthetic. Adobe Creative Cloud, Figma, and modern front-end tools are fine; just keep the workflow professional and streamlined. If this sounds like your craft, let’s create a brand that looks as expensive as it feels.

title: Streetwear Designer, Content Creator & Brand Growth Strategist (Long-Term Opportunity)
desc: We are building a premium streetwear fashion startup focused on exclusivity, storytelling, culture, and community. Our goal is not simply to sell clothing. We want to build a brand that people actively follow, engage with, talk about, and wait for before every product release. We are looking for a creative and ambitious individual who understands modern streetwear culture, Gen Z consumer psychology, fashion design, content creation, and organic brand growth. This is a long-term opportunity for someone who wants to help build a fashion brand from the ground up. --- ## Our Vision We are building the brand around a limited-drop model. Instead of constantly releasing products, we will launch carefully planned collections and exclusive drops. Key principles: • Limited quantities per design • Approximately 50 units per design • No restocks once sold out • Exclusive and collectible products • Story-driven launches • Community-first growth • Organic hype generation We want customers to feel that owning a piece from a drop is special because once it is gone, it is gone forever. Because of this, product design, storytelling, and community building are equally important. --- ## What We Need Most The most important part of this role is product design. We are looking for someone who can create original and premium streetwear concepts that feel exclusive, culturally relevant, and desirable. However, we do not believe great products alone are enough. The ideal candidate should also understand how to turn the design process into engaging content and help build excitement around future drops. We are looking for someone who can contribute across: • Product Design • Collection Development • Creative Direction • Content Creation • Instagram Reels • Brand Storytelling • Community Building • Organic Growth The product always comes first, but we want someone who understands how great brands use content and storytelling to create demand around great products. --- ## Key Responsibilities ### Product Design (Primary Focus) • Design original premium streetwear concepts • Create complete collection and drop concepts • Develop graphics, typography systems, symbols, and visual assets • Create front, back, sleeve, neck label, hang tag, and packaging concepts • Produce professional product mockups • Research trends while maintaining originality • Help build a unique visual language for the brand • Contribute ideas for future collections and drops ### Content Creation & Storytelling • Create Instagram Reel concepts • Develop content around the design process • Create behind-the-scenes content ideas • Suggest product reveal concepts • Develop storytelling frameworks around collections • Turn product development into engaging social content ### Brand Growth & Community Building • Contribute ideas for organic Instagram growth • Help build anticipation before every drop • Develop hype campaigns and launch strategies • Suggest ways to increase engagement and community participation • Help create demand before products are released • Research successful streetwear and fashion marketing strategies --- ## What Success Looks Like Our goal is not simply to gain followers. We want to: • Build a loyal community • Create highly desirable products • Generate excitement before every launch • Make every drop feel exclusive • Build anticipation and demand organically • Create content that people actively follow and engage with --- ## Ideal Candidate We are looking for someone who: • Has a genuine passion for streetwear and fashion culture • Understands Gen Z consumer behavior • Has strong design skills and creative thinking • Understands what makes fashion brands desirable • Understands Instagram, TikTok, and short-form content • Can create original ideas consistently • Has strong taste and attention to detail • Follows global streetwear and fashion trends • Is ambitious and eager to grow with a brand --- ## Preferred Skills Experience in one or more of the following: • Streetwear design • Fashion design • Apparel design • Graphic design • Branding • Content creation • Social media growth • Creative storytelling • Video editing --- ## Application Requirements To apply, please provide: ### 1. Portfolio Share your strongest fashion, streetwear, apparel, branding, design, or content work. ### 2. Relevant Experience Tell us about projects, brands, or pages you have worked on and your role in those projects. ### 3. Design Challenge If you were building a premium streetwear brand focused on limited drops, how would you create products that feel exclusive and desirable? ### 4. Growth Challenge Imagine you are launching a completely new streetwear brand with: • 0 followers • 0 customers • 1 upcoming drop How would you create enough excitement and demand organically before launch? ### 5. Drop Strategy Challenge Each design is limited to approximately 50 units and will never be restocked. Describe how you would build hype and anticipation over a 30-60 day period before the drop. ### 6. Content Challenge Share 5 Instagram Reel ideas you would create before the first launch. ### 7. Brand Analysis Name 3 fashion or streetwear brands whose content and marketing strategy you admire and explain what makes them successful. --- ## Compensation Budget: ₹10,000/month This is an early-stage opportunity with the potential for long-term collaboration. As the brand grows, there may be opportunities for increased responsibilities, compensation, and involvement in future collections, campaigns, and creative initiatives. --- ## Important Applications containing copied work, template-based designs, generic AI-generated responses, or low-effort submissions will not be considered. We are looking for originality, creativity, strong design ability, strategic thinking, and a genuine understanding of modern streetwear culture. If you are passionate about fashion, design, storytelling, content creation, and helping build a premium streetwear brand from the ground up, we would love to hear from you.

title: Book Cover Design
budget: Hourly Entry Level Est. time: Less than 1 month, Less than 30 hrs/week
desc: Here's a fuller brief for the designer: Book Cover Design Brief - Speedrunning Capitalism Hello, I have finished writing my book and require a front cover designed, with a back cover to follow once the physical edition is confirmed. The Book Title: Speedrunning Capitalism Subtitle: How Business Acquisition Accelerates Your Wealth Author: Brandon Lipman Speedrunning Capitalism is a business acquisition guide for the middle class. It's direct, practical and unapologetic. Not a traditional business book - no corporate tone, no fluff. Think closer to Alex Hormozi's visual aesthetic than a Harvard Business Review publication. Current Draft I have attached a draft cover I've put together in Canva. You're welcome to improve on this layout or take it in a completely different creative direction - I'm open to both. The Image The current draft uses an arrow breaking through a horizontal line. The concept is: something fast, precise and powerful breaking through a ceiling or orbit. The arrow works as a metaphor for both a traditional arrow and a rocket - deliberately ambiguous. I specifically don't want a rocket. Rockets are overused on business books and too literal. The arrow-breaking-through-a-line concept captures the same idea without being cliche. If you have a stronger creative idea that captures the same energy - acceleration, breaking through, upward trajectory - I'm open to seeing it. Visual elements may include weathered white wood textures, picket fence borders, surfboards, sandy backgrounds, rope details, vintage beach signage, and hand-painted type accents. The overall feel should be warm, playful, and feminine, cottage-core meets beach girl. The catalog includes: - Cover page - Retailer brand intro (wholesale-focused) - Founder story page - Wholesale pricing & terms page - Size guide - approx 15-20 product pages (mix of lifestyle photography + flat-lay illustrations + product details) - Quick order form - Back cover with contact info What We're Looking For You are a strong fit if you: - Have a portfolio that includes fashion or apparel catalogs, lookbooks, or linesheets (this is required — please include relevant samples) - Understand the difference between a consumer-facing lookbook and a wholesale buyer catalog, and can design for both function and aesthetic - Have experience designing with photography — you know how to make lifestyle images and flat-lay product shots work together on a page - Are confident with texture-heavy, editorial design (not just clean minimal layouts) - Can deliver print-ready AND screen-optimized PDF versions - Are comfortable with a revision rounds based on feedback Software: Adobe InDesign, Canva Pro or Affinity Publisher also acceptable if your portfolio demonstrates strong output. Deliverables - 1 concept page (single product spread) for approval before full build - Full catalog in the approved style - Print-ready PDF (300 DPI, CMYK, with bleed) - Screen/email PDF (RGB, compressed under 10MB) - All working files handed over on completion -Editable version so my team can add new pages when launching new products Project Details - Timeline: Concept page within 5 business days of hire; full catalog within 3 weeks of concept approval - Budget: Please submit your fixed-price quote with your proposal - Revisions: 3 rounds included How to Apply To be considered, your proposal must include: 1. 2–3 portfolio samples of fashion catalogs, apparel lookbooks, or wholesale linesheets you have designed 2. A brief note (3–5 sentences) describing your approach to the beach town aesthetic and how you'd apply it to a swimwear catalog 3. title: Boutique Hotel Sales Document Creation
desc: I need to create a selling document for a Boutique Hotel. There are a lot of statistics to crunch and some basic real estate key points to put the emphasis on. I will share many photos to choose from and the whole story line is already established; Page 1 — Cover Title: Le Pleasant Hotel and Monte Verde Restaurant Page 2 — Executive summary Asset: boutique heritage hotel (1902), 10 keys, café/restaurant, multifunction room, signature terrace in the heart of Sutton. Positioning: strong online reputation (Google/Tripadvisor), leisure, foodie and business clientele. Recent KPIs: 2024 — occupancy 71.5%, ADR ~$205, RevPAR ~$147. Trajectory: Revenue 2026 ~$907.6k, 2027 ~$960.3k; 2027 net result ~$120.8k; target RevPAR $160–$170. Investment plan: $370.7k (junior suites, workstations, dinner service kitchen, business lounge, terrace, smart thermostats, exterior). Value: indicative price $2.20–$2.60M; asking price $2.45M; assessed (tax roll) value $899k. Partnerships: Mont Sutton, Balnea, Route des Vins, Vélo Québec, Saveurs des Cantons, etc. Page 3 — About us To be provided Page 4 — The asset Area: 6,066 sq ft Keys: 10 (planned conversion: 2 junior suites king; 5 rooms with workspaces) Spaces: café/restaurant (Monté Verdé), multifunction lounge/room, garden terrace Location: Sutton village center, Brome‑Missisquoi RCM (Estrie / Eastern Townships) Highlights: 1902 architecture, heritage woodwork and staircase, highly attractive terrace Photos: guestroom, staircase, bar/dining room Page 5 — Recent performance 2023–2024 summary: Occupancy: 67.5% (2023) → 71.5% (2024) ADR: ~$197 → ~$205 RevPAR: ~$133 → ~$147 Chart: Occupancy/ADR/RevPAR trend (insert monthly data) 2024 visitation (total 6,200): local 2,200; regional 800; Quebec 1,800; Canada 600; United States 600; other international 200 Pie chart: visitor origin breakdown Page 6 — Forecast P&L (extract) Total revenue: 2025 $834.1k | 2026 $907.6k | 2027 $960.3k COGS: 2026 ~$569.7k | 2027 ~$607.6k Gross margin: 2026 ~$338.0k | 2027 ~$352.7k Operating expenses (OPEX): 2026 ~$227.1k | 2027 ~$231.9k Net result: 2025 ~$77.4k | 2026 ~$110.8k | 2027 ~$120.8k Waterfall chart: 2025 → 2027 improvement Page 7 — Market and seasonality Four‑season drivers: ski/mountain, cycling/Route Verte, wine route, Balnea spa, business tourism. Strategy: extended stays and digital nomads (workstations), retreats/meetings (multifunction room), evening F&B upgrade. Page 8 — Investment plan ($370,700) in progress Safety/quality: external fire escape $30k; fire‑rated reception doors $8k; signage $5k Rooms: junior suites $30k; carpets/soundproofing $45k; furniture + workstations $50k Evening F&B: kitchen reconfiguration $15k; equipment $15k Spaces: multifunction lounge + dining room + terrace $25k; exterior cladding repairs/paint $60k Comfort/energy: smart thermostats $5k Studies/plans/design: $30k Contingency: $47.7k; Public art: $5k CAPEX table: detail the above items Page 10 — Restaurant experience: Monté Verdé Positioning: Mediterranean cuisine led by Sandro Meffé (former Il Duetto, 1986–2013) Service: Breakfast: Wednesday to Sunday, 8:00–10:00 Lunch: Friday to Sunday, 11:00–15:00 Menus: fresh, local, seasonal; majority gluten‑free options Nods to Il Duetto: signature dishes; desserts by Mary Hastings; “Le Nid” iconic dessert on the menu Renovated terrace: multiple tables, lounge areas, large umbrellas; interior chic yet unpretentious Pull‑quote: “I’ve trained my body for this hard drug called restaurants…” — Sandro Meffé Link/QR: restaurantmonteverde.com Monte Verde logo in section header Page 11 — Valuation and methods Indicative price: $2.20–$2.60M; Asking: $2.45M Assessed value: $899k (fiscal reference) Sustaining CAPEX: $10k/year NOI capitalization: market cap rate 9.5–10.5% → consistent with $2.20–$2.60M range Five‑year DCF: 12–13% discount; 9.5–10.0% exit cap → $2.25–$2.55M Sensitivities: ±$10 ADR ≈ ±$25–35k NOI ±1 pt occupancy ≈ ±$10–15k NOI ±50 bps cap rate ≈ ±$120–140k value Chart: value vs cap rate sensitivity (8.5%–10.5%) Page 12 — Process and terms Perimeter: operating hotel + F&B + terrace + multifunction room + FF&E + customary closing inventories. Process: NDA → dataroom → site visit → LOI → due diligence → closing Legal note: information indicative and non‑contractual; projections based on assumptions subject to change Page 13+ — Appendices Monthly occupancy tables 2023–2024 (ADR/RevPAR) Forecast P&L 2025–2027 (extracts) Detailed CAPEX and timeline (2025–2026) Selected links/partnerships Photo gallery: facade, entrance, terrace (several angles), dining/bar, room, staircase

title: Cafe Beverage Sticker Redesign
desc: I need our existing beverage sticker taken from “good enough” to truly shelf-worthy. The café has three bottled items—Cold Coffee, Cold Cocoa and an Ice Tea—and each share the same core layout today. I want a full refresh that keeps the size and basic die-cut shape but upgrades everything else: colour palette, font choice and visual accents. Font: the current typeface feels dated and heavy. I’d like something cleaner, balanced and legible at arm’s length when the bottles sit in a cooler. Colour scheme: each flavour should feel distinct yet obviously from the same brand family. You’re free to propose new tones that complement a warm café identity while still popping against condensation and low-light fridges. Imagery: I’m leaning toward abstract graphics rather than literal product photos or stereotypical café icons. Think subtle patterns, shapes or textures that evoke cool refreshment and give each sticker memorable shelf presence. Deliverables • Three print-ready sticker files (AI or layered PSD plus press-quality PDF) • One mock-up of each design on a bottle for quick stakeholder approval • Colour, font and graphic usage guide so we can replicate the look across future SKUs I’ll provide the current artwork, logo files and exact die-line the moment we start. Let me know your timeline, any clarifying questions and a quick note on your typical revision process so we can keep momentum once the first concepts land.
mcp_workflow_v1: search_design (Express beverage-sticker template, clean modern, warm cafe palette) -> asset_add_file (logo) -> fill_text (product/flavour names) -> image_crop_to_bounds (keep existing die-cut size/shape) -> asset_preview_file  [x3 SKUs] -> document_render_vector / document_convert_pdf (press-ready)

title: Catchy Water Bottle Label Design
desc: I need a fresh set of water-bottle labels that feel playful and instantly catch the eye of brewery and restaurant patrons. The look we’re after is colorful, bright, and bold— nothing muted or vintage—so please lean into lively palettes, dynamic shapes, and fun typography. The label must keep our brand logo and the product name front and center; everything else can be treated as supportive design elements. I’ll supply logo files, dielines, and a short creative brief the moment we kick off. From there, I’d like to collaborate through two or three quick review rounds to make sure the artwork reflects the quirky, upbeat personality we’ve established for our clients. Deliverables • Two initial concepts presented as flat mock-ups • One final print-ready file (AI or layered PSD + PDF) sized to the provided dieline • All linked fonts and graphics in an organized package I work in Adobe Illustrator, so an .ai master file keeps hand-offs simple, but if you prefer Photoshop that’s fine as long as layers remain editable. If bright, bold, and playful label design is in your wheelhouse, let’s talk and get these bottles looking great.

Preferred Experience Meta / Facebook / Instagram ad creative design Food & beverage marketing or e-commerce ads Direct-response advertising understanding Strong grasp of CTR / conversion-focused creative principles Application Requirement Please include examples of previous Meta ad creatives or performance marketing work, especially in food, beverage, or consumer product niches. title: Premium Investor Pitch Deck + One-Page Brief for FanStreak
desc: I need a professional investor-ready and creator-onboarding-ready pitch deck + one-page startup brief for my startup FanStreak. Live website: https://fan-streak.vercel.app/ Please open the live website yourself, explore the product, take high-quality screenshots, and use those screenshots inside the pitch deck. I do not want to manually prepare screenshots unless absolutely required. FanStreak is a creator-fan monetization platform where fans support creators, build streaks, unlock fan levels, compete on leaderboards, buy FanStreak Passport, join Creator Drops, and help creators build BrandScore / brand proof through real fan activity. The deck should be suitable for: - Investors - Creator partners - Advisors - Startup platforms - Early onboarding creators This should look like a premium startup pitch deck made by a professional agency, not a generic AI/Canva presentation. Deliverables: 1. Editable PowerPoint pitch deck (.pptx) 2. PDF version of the pitch deck 3. One-page startup brief in PDF 4. Editable source file if made in Canva / Figma / Pitch / PowerPoint 5. Mention if any fonts/icons/images used are not free/commercial-safe Design style: - Dark premium startup theme - Black / deep charcoal background - Orange, pink, purple gradient accents - Clean modern typography - Rounded premium cards - Subtle glow effects - Modern creator economy / fintech / premium tech startup feel - Investor-grade visual hierarchy - No childish design - No cheap neon/gaming style - No cluttered slides - No random stock UI - No generic template look Please capture screenshots from the live site: 1. Homepage 2. Public creator page 3. Support levels section 4. Leaderboards section 5. Creator Drop Engine section 6. FanStreak Passport section 7. Live Passport page 8. Creator Studio page if accessible 9. Creator Pro Studio / BrandScore section If any page requires login or access, ask me before using it. Do not ask for source code or private credentials. Core positioning: FanStreak turns creator fandom into direct revenue, streaks, identity, leaderboards, Passport, creator drops, and brand proof. One-line explanation: FanStreak helps creators convert loyal fan attention into direct income, fan identity, community competition, and future brand collaboration proof. Pitch deck should be 25–35 slides, or as many as needed. It should cover: 1. Cover Slide FanStreak title, tagline, premium product visual. Suggested tagline: “Turn fandom into revenue, identity, and brand proof.” 2. One-Line Pitch Explain FanStreak in one powerful sentence. 3. Problem: Creators Creators may have followers, views, comments, and DMs, but they still struggle with predictable income, loyal fan monetization, and proving real audience quality to brands. 4. Problem: Fans Fans support creators emotionally, but most platforms do not give them recognition, status, streaks, identity, or visibility inside the creator’s community. 5. Existing Platforms Gap Compare Instagram, YouTube, Twitch, Patreon, Super Chat, Buy Me a Coffee, and UPI. Explain gaps: algorithm dependency, weak fan identity, no streak/status loop, weak loyalty proof, and limited brand proof. 6. FanStreak Solution Fan support becomes streak, rank, badge, identity, Creator Drop participation, Passport, and brand proof. 7. Product Ecosystem Show: fan support, fan levels, streaks, leaderboards, Creator Drop Engine, FanStreak Passport, Creator Studio, Creator Pro Studio, BrandScore, and future Live Rooms / Arena. 8. Fan Journey Discover creator → support → build streak → rank on leaderboard → unlock fan level → buy Passport → join drops → become visible. 9. Creator Journey Join FanStreak → create creator page → share link → receive fan support → track loyal fans → run drops → build BrandScore → use proof for brand opportunities. 10. Public Creator Page Use real screenshot. Explain creator profile, social links, support levels, leaderboards, Creator Drop, and Passport. 11. Support Levels Explain: ₹9 Starter Fan ₹19 Active Fan ₹49 Loyal Fan ₹599 Elite Fan ₹999 VIP Fan Custom Power Supporter Fans are not just paying; they are buying visible fan status. 12. Streaks and Leaderboards Explain: - Highest Streak - Most Loyal Fans - Highest Paid Show how this creates repeat engagement and fan competition. 13. Creator Drop Engine Limited-time fan challenges that create urgency, competition, and stronger fan activity. Examples: 24-hour drop, top supporters highlighted, Passport fans get priority visibility, fans compete for recognition. 14. FanStreak Passport A ₹99 premium fan identity upgrade. Benefits: - Premium digital Passport card - QR-linked live fan profile - Passport badge - Fan Wall visibility - Creator Drop priority alerts - Future meetup priority consideration Important: Passport does not buy leaderboard rank. It is not pay-to-win. Rankings are based on real support and consistency. 15. Passport UI Slide Use screenshot/mockup of Passport card. Show masked preview before purchase and real details after purchase. 16. Creator Studio Use screenshot. Explain creator dashboard, fan analytics, leaderboards, rewards settings, future payouts, and creator page management. 17. Creator Pro Studio Explain Creator Pro Studio as a way for creators to turn fan loyalty into brand value. Includes: - FanStreak BrandScore - Collab Proof Profile - Media Kit Generator - Brand Request Inbox - Sponsored Fan Challenges - Deep fan/revenue analytics 18. BrandScore Explain BrandScore as proof based on active supporters, Passport fans, repeat support, longest streak, support volume, and Creator Drop activity. Core line: “Brands do not just need creators with followers. They need creators with fans who act.” 19. Business Model Revenue streams: - Platform/service fee on fan support - FanStreak Passport - Creator Pro subscription - Sponsored Creator Drops - Brand campaign tools - Sponsored Fan Challenges - Future Live Rooms - Featured placements later 20. Creator-Friendly Economics Explain that creators should feel they get maximum benefit from direct fan support. FanStreak monetizes through tools, service layers, Passport, Creator Pro, and brand campaigns. 21. Why Fans Will Pay Repeatedly Be honest: fans will not pay repeatedly for donation alone. They pay when support creates status, streak, rank, recognition, creator attention, fan identity, urgency, and community visibility. 22. Why Creators Will Promote FanStreak Creators promote FanStreak because it gives direct fan income, loyal fan data, fan engagement loops, Creator Drops, BrandScore, media kit proof, and future brand opportunities. 23. Competitive Comparison Create a comparison matrix: FanStreak vs Instagram Subscriptions vs YouTube Memberships vs Twitch vs Patreon vs Super Chat vs UPI. Compare on direct support, streaks, fan identity, leaderboards, drops, Passport, BrandScore, brand proof, and creator-owned community. 24. Market Opportunity Explain creator economy growth, micro/mid creators, Indian digital payments, fan communities, and brand collaborations. Do not make fake claims. Mark numbers as estimates unless verified. 25. Go-To-Market Plan Initial launch: - Start with 5–10 pilot creators - Focus on creators with loyal fan communities - Priority categories: comedy, fitness, gaming, regional creators, meme/community pages, college creators - Set up creator pages manually - Run first Creator Drops - Track fan support, Passport interest, and repeat engagement 26. Creator Onboarding Plan Invite creator → set up creator page → creator shares link → fans support/build streaks → creator runs drop → collect feedback → improve product → use success cases. 27. Current MVP Status Be honest: FanStreak has a working MVP built and deployed on Vercel. Features visible: - Homepage - Public creator pages - Creator Studio - Support levels - Leaderboards - Creator Drop Engine - FanStreak Passport - Creator Pro Studio / BrandScore Also mention that real payment integration, production backend logic, and full security hardening are part of the next roadmap. 28. Backend / Security / Production Readiness Mention planned priorities: production Firestore rules, admin protection, creator dashboard ownership protection, payment verification via backend/webhooks, fraud prevention, payout records, rate limits, monitoring, and moving demo/localStorage logic to backend. 29. Risks Be brutally honest: - Fans may not pay repeatedly without creator push - Creators may not promote if revenue is weak - Payment fees may affect micro-payments - Fake creator risk exists - Security must be production-grade before scale - Brand trust must be built carefully 30. Risk Mitigation Start with pilot creators, test fan conversion before scaling, use streaks/drops/passport to improve repeat support, verify creators, use backend payment verification, keep brand-safe rules, and do manual onboarding first. 31. Funding / Support Needed Funding/support will help with backend development, payment gateway integration, creator onboarding, security hardening, UI polish, pilot marketing, legal/payment compliance, and brand/creator outreach. 32. Final Closing Slide Message: FanStreak is not just a support link. It is a creator fan-economy layer where support becomes streak, identity, proof, and revenue. One-page brief: Create a clean, premium one-page brief that explains: - What FanStreak is - The problem - The solution - Key features - Current MVP status - Business model - Creator benefits - Fan benefits - Roadmap - Contact / next step section The one-page brief should be suitable for emailing investors, creators, startup platforms, and advisors. Please share previous startup pitch deck samples before starting. I need someone with strong storytelling and premium presentation design skills, not just basic slide formatting.

budget: Fixed price Intermediate Est. budget: $15.00
desc: I am looking for an expert logo designer to finalise the master logo for my business. The creative concept is already complete. I need the following for logo design: • High-resolution SVG files (optimized for web) • PNG files with transparent background in multiple sizes (512px, 1024px, 2000px, and 5000px+) • EPS and PDF versions (for print) • High-resolution JPG versions • Editable source files (.AI or Figma preferred) 2. Need these variations for the same logo: • Full colour version • Black version • White version (for dark backgrounds) • Monochrome / grayscale version • Vertical / Stacked version • Icon-only version • Text-only version 3. I will provide: • Exact color codes (Hex) • Font name(s), weight and styles used in the logo • Minimum clear space / exclusion zone and minimum size recommendations for logo design 4. Other Important Points • Please confirm I will receive full commercial ownership and rights to the final designs. • Also let me know the timeline for final delivery.
mcp_workflow_v1: asset_add_file (upload the client's finalized 'Flow and Circularity' emblem raster) -> image_vectorize (raster -> clean vector paths of the approved mark) -> document_render_vector (export master as AI, EPS, PDF, SVG, and JPG) -> image_remove_background (ensure transparent edges) -> document_render_vector / image_crop_and_resize (export transparent PNGs at the required 512 / 1024 / 2000 / 5000px sizes) -> image_apply_color_overlay + image_apply_monochromatic_tint (produce the black, white, and grayscale/monochrome color versions) -> asset_preview_file

TEXT STYLE: Very short text (2–6 words ideal) Large and instantly readable on mobile Use emotionally charged phrases Text should create curiosity, urgency, or revelation Examples: “The Portal Has Opened” “You’re Awakening” “This Changes Everything” “The Energy Shift Is Here” “Why You Feel Different” “The Divine Feminine Returns” COMPOSITION: Preferred: Large close-up portrait Strong focal point Minimal distractions One dominant emotional idea Cinematic cropping Clean separation between subject/background CTR PSYCHOLOGY: Thumbnail should trigger: curiosity emotional resonance spiritual significance transformation exclusivity mystery “I NEED to know this” It should feel like: “This video contains hidden wisdom specifically for me.” BRAND CONSISTENCY: All thumbnails should look like part of one recognizable universe. title: Photo Colour Correction for Mood
desc: I have a batch of photos that need a careful colour treatment to evoke a distinct mood and artistic feel—not simply boosting saturation, but balancing tones, shadows and highlights so the viewer senses the intended atmosphere at first glance. The raw or high-resolution originals will be supplied immediately after we agree on the job. Please work in Photoshop, Lightroom, Capture One or any comparable professional tool; non-destructive editing is essential because I may ask for minor tweaks once I review your first pass. Turnaround is tight: I’d like the fully corrected images back within a couple of days. Deliverables • Colour-graded, high-resolution JPEGs (sRGB) • Layered PSD or TIFF files with adjustments intact • Brief note on the primary adjustments or LUTs used I will sign off once the images share a coherent mood, preserve natural skin tones where relevant, and display no clipping or banding. If this matches your skill set and timeframe, let’s get started right away.
mcp_workflow_v1: asset_add_file (ingest each supplied RAW/high-res original) -> image_apply_auto_tone (neutralise exposure baseline before stylising) -> image_adjust_color_temperature (set the mood's warm/cool cast) -> image_adjust_highlights + image_adjust_dark_portions (balance shadows/highlights to avoid clipping & banding) -> image_adjust_hsl (shape the atmospheric palette while protecting skin hues) -> image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation (lift mood without crushing skin tones) -> image_list_presets/image_apply_preset (save the first-pass look and reapply across the rest of the batch for a coherent grade) -> asset_preview_file (client review of first pass for minor tweaks)

Let me know if additional specs are useful.

Here’s what I need from you: • Final images returned in both high-resolution JPEG (sRGB) and PNG with transparent edges preserved. • Consistent pure-white background (RGB 255-255-255) across the whole batch so they look uniform when placed side by side. • File names kept identical to the originals for easy replacement. • A brief sample set first (5–10 images) so I can confirm the lighting and edge work before you process the full run. I’ll supply the RAW or high-res JPEG source files via a cloud link as soon as we start. Fast, precise masking and a natural feathered edge around hair and fine details are important, and I’m aiming for a quick turnaround once the sample is approved.

title: Poster Pengumuman Informasi Menarik

Apa yang saya butuhkan: • Satu konsep poster original yang langsung menyampaikan pesan utama pengumuman. • Ukuran dasar A3; versi A4 ikut disertakan. • File akhir berformat .AI atau .PSD beserta PDF siap cetak (300 dpi, CMYK). • Versi web (JPEG/PNG, 1080 px sisi terpanjang) agar mudah dibagikan di media sosial. • Tipografi jelas, warna kontras, dan ruang untuk memasukkan teks pengumuman final. Gaya desain sepenuhnya terbuka—minimalis, ilustratif, ataupun fotografi—selama tetap informatif dan mudah dibaca dari jarak pandang poster biasa. Sertakan mock-up penempatan (mis. di dinding atau papan pengumuman) agar saya bisa membayangkan hasil akhirnya. Kriteria penilaian: 1. Keterbacaan pesan inti dalam 3 detik pertama. 2. Orisinalitas layout dan elemen grafis. 3. Kesesuaian warna serta tipografi dengan tema informatif. 4. Kemudahan adaptasi ke ukuran atau bahasa lain.
