I need a polished professional to make the full system visually consistent, premium, readable, and launch-ready. ## Project Overview This is a nonfiction book system for physicians. It includes: * Main book * Companion playbook * Workbook / calculator companion * Infographic library * Covers and back covers * Chapter openers * Part divider pages * Table of contents * Figures, callout boxes, and captions * QR codes and website references * Final PDF and DOCX files The audience is physicians and high-income professionals. The design should feel premium, clean, restrained, and trustworthy. ## Design Style Required The final product should feel: * Professional * Premium * Clean * Elegant * Physician-appropriate * Easy to read * Consistent across all files * Modern but not trendy * Polished but not overdesigned * Educational, not salesy I do not want the final product to look like a generic Canva lead magnet. It should feel like a serious, high-quality nonfiction education system. ## What I Need Done Please help finalize: * Interior layout * Margins and page setup * Headers and footers * Page numbers * Table of contents formatting * Chapter title and subtitle styling * Part divider pages * Figure placement * Figure captions * Block quote / maxim styling * Callout boxes * QR code placement and readability * Website URL placement and consistency * Cover and back cover attachment if needed * PDF export * Visual QA * Consistency across the full product system ## Final QA Requirements The final files should be checked for: * Correct page order * Correct table of contents * No clipped text * No overlapping graphics * No broken tables * No distorted images * No blurry QR codes * No missing page numbers * Consistent margins * Consistent fonts * Consistent heading hierarchy * Clean spacing around quotes, figures, and callouts * Correct front matter and back matter * Professional PDF output * Digital readability * Print-readiness if applicable ## Deliverables Please provide: 1. Final designed DOCX files where applicable. 2. Final clean digital PDF files. 3. Print-ready PDF files if applicable. 4. Updated graphics or infographic files if needed. 5. A short design QA report listing: * What you fixed * What you changed * Any remaining issues * Any print or export concerns ## Preferred Workflow / Milestones I prefer fixed-price milestones: **Milestone 1:** Review files and produce a short design/formatting audit. **Milestone 2:** Format and polish the main book. **Milestone 3:** Format and polish companion materials. **Milestone 4:** Final PDF export and visual QA. **Milestone 5:** One revision round after author review. ## Ideal Candidate You should have experience with: * Nonfiction book interiors * Workbooks or professional guides * PDF design * Print-ready formatting * DOCX formatting * Infographics or educational visuals * Adobe InDesign, Affinity Publisher, Microsoft Word, Canva Pro, or similar tools Experience with medical, finance, business, coaching, or professional education content is a plus. ## What I Am Not Looking For I am not looking for: * A complete brand redesign unless truly necessary * Decorative design that weakens readability * Generic template formatting * A rushed PDF export * Someone who cannot perform careful visual QA * Someone who ignores page order, QR codes, captions, or TOC accuracy ## Confidentiality The files include unpublished book content and personal/professional stories. Confidentiality is required. I may request an NDA before sharing the complete files. ## Please Include In Your Proposal Please answer these questions: 1. Can you share examples of nonfiction book interiors you designed? 2. Have you designed workbooks, guides, or educational PDFs? 3. Which software do you use? 4. Can you work with DOCX and PDF files? 5. Can you create both digital and print-ready PDFs? 6. Can you check QR codes and visual layout issues? 7. What is your estimated timeline? 8. What fixed price would you quote? 9. Are you willing to complete a small paid sample layout first? I am looking for someone who can take an already strong book system and make it look polished, premium, consistent, and ready for launch.

title: Editorial eBook Designer for 2 Premium Health Books (German · A4 PDF)
budget: Hourly: $5.00 - $20.00 Intermediate Est. time: 1 to 3 months, Less than 30 hrs/w
desc: # Editorial Book Designer for Premium Wellness eBook Bundle (German) ## About the Project We manage the brand of Dr. Maren Kemper — a chemist and microbiome researcher, supplement founder, published author, and educator with a large following in the gut health and nutrition space. Her book Der Ballaststoff-Effekt was published by Südwest, one of Germany's leading publishers. We're currently producing a bundle of two eBooks, targeted for launch in July, and are looking for a designer to create the core design system and fully typeset both books. --- ## The Aesthetic (Most Important) Editorial, minimal, and premium. Think Kinfolk magazine meets high-end wellness brands such as Aesop and The Nue Co. This should not feel like a typical health guide. No clip art, no loud infographics, and no overly commercial design elements. ### Design Direction - Quality benchmark: the design should feel as refined as Dr. Kemper's published book Der Ballaststoff-Effekt (we'll share it as a reference) - Color palette: black, white, and warm beige - Typography: - PP Editorial New (or similar high-contrast editorial serif) for headlines - Poppins (or similar clean sans-serif) for body copy - Overall feel: - Beautifully typeset editorial publication - Calm, confident, science-led, and warm - Generous whitespace and strong typography - Premium wellness rather than "health guide" --- ## Scope & Deliverables ### 1. Core Design System Create the foundation for both books, including: - Master layout grid - Chapter opener template - Typographic hierarchy - Image integration style - Callout components - Recipe layouts - Footnotes and source styling - Other recurring design elements ### 2. Full Typesetting — Book 1 "Schlank durch Darm" - Content is finalized - Approximately 60–80 pages - We'll provide: - Final text - An AI-generated layout draft that serves only as a content map Your design will replace the draft entirely. ### 3. Full Typesetting — Book 2 "50 Alltagsrezepte" - Approximately 80–110 pages - 50 recipes plus introduction and supporting content - Text is currently being finalized ### 4. eBook Cover - Front cover only - Digital eBook format (no spine, flaps, or print jacket) - We'll share visual references and cover directions that Dr. Kemper likes --- ## Paid Sample Before Full Project Before committing to the full project, we'd like to start with a small paid sample to ensure the design direction is aligned. The sample should include: - One front-cover concept - One chapter opener - One content or recipe spread This is paid work. We do not ask for unpaid spec work. Once approved, we'll move directly into the full design system and typesetting project. --- ## Format & Assets - Format: A4, digital-first PDF - Please let us know whether you can also provide a print-ready version as an optional add-on - We provide all imagery: - Maren portraits - 50 recipe photos - Consistent visual style throughout - No stock image sourcing required ### Language The books are in German. You do not need to speak German fluently, but you should be comfortable handling German typography correctly, including: - Umlauts (ä, ö, ü) - ß - Proper hyphenation and text flow --- ## What to Include in Your Proposal Please include: 1. 2–3 relevant portfolio samples (editorial books, magazines, long-form layouts, or premium wellness publications) 2. Your fixed price and timeline for: - The paid sample - The full project 3. A realistic production timeline toward a July launch 4. Your software stack (InDesign, Figma, etc.) 5. Whether editable source files are included in the final delivery We're looking for someone with a strong editorial eye who can create something that feels timeless, premium, and worthy of a published author brand.

The profile is for a GCC-based founder/operator. The CV positions me as Co-Founder and General Manager of MinDev, a UAE-incorporated venture studio operating across Saudi Arabia and the GCC. The work spans AI-enabled software delivery, regulated software platforms, enterprise client engagement, venture incubation, equity-for-services structures, and Saudi market execution. The current CV already includes the key experience, including: * Co-Founder and General Manager of MinDev * Venture studio operations across custom development, internal ventures, and equity-for-services partnerships * Enterprise software and regulated platform work for Saudi-focused markets * Selected engagements involving Daify, a KSA-native property management system for the short-term rental market * Alkhaleej Training & Education / Gordon Ramsay Academy KSA digital platform work * Coordination between Saudi and London-side stakeholders * HyperPay/payment integration, third-party dependencies, go-live readiness, and delivery coordination * Earlier commercial leadership experience in Saudi luxury retail and hospitality-related advisory work * EHL Hospitality Business School education What I need from you: 1. Refine the wording so it sounds senior, polished, and credible without exaggeration. 2. Improve the visual hierarchy, spacing, alignment, typography, and general layout. 3. Keep it to one page if possible. 4. Make it feel premium and executive, but not over-designed or gimmicky. 5. Improve readability for both human readers and professional screening. 6. Make the experience section easier to scan, especially the distinction between roles, selected ventures, and selected engagements. 7. Ensure the wording is commercially sharp and suitable for GCC/Saudi/UAE business environments. 8. Flag any claims, titles, or wording that may sound inflated, unclear, or risky. 9. Recommend small wording improvements where needed, especially around MinDev, Daify, Alkhaleej, and Gordon Ramsay Academy KSA. 10. Deliver an editable version and a polished PDF version. Important: I do not want a generic CV rewrite. I want refinement of an already-developed executive CV. The tone should be founder/operator, venture studio, enterprise software, and GCC-commercial — not a junior job-seeker CV. The final output should make the profile feel sharper, cleaner, more credible, and more boardroom-ready.

title: Marketing Collateral & Brand Assets
budget: Hourly: $15.00 - $100.00 Intermediate Est. time: Less than 1 month, Less than 30
desc: Project Brief: Marketing Collateral & Brand Assets Deliverables: Branded Presentation Folder, One-Page Service Overview, and Branded Sample Case Notes Template. 1. Project Overview & Branding Guidelines The organization provides premier, high-quality case management services, with a significant focus on complex motor vehicle accident (MVA) cases. Our work is professional, meticulous, and deeply supportive. All design deliverables must reflect this premium standard. For branding reference, color palettes, typography, and visual tone, please utilize our website. The aesthetic should be clean, modern, trustworthy, and polished. 2. Detailed Deliverable Specifications Deliverable 1: Branded Two-Pocket Presentation Folder • Purpose: To serve as the primary physical package handed to attorneys, medical providers, and clients containing our marketing materials, assessments, and reports. • Physical Specifications: Standard 9" x 12" presentation folder with two interior pockets. The right-hand interior pocket must feature standard die-cut slits to hold a business card. • Design Requirements: • Front Cover: Minimalist, clean, and high-impact. Must prominently feature the logo, brand colors, and core tagline/aesthetic. • Back Cover: Clean design containing essential company contact information (Website, Phone, Email, Office Address) and a clean layout. • Interior Pockets: Can utilize a complementary brand color or subtle geometric/abstract pattern that aligns with the website's visual assets. • Execution Note: Please deliver print-ready files (PDF/X-1a) Deliverable 2: One-Page Business Service Overview • Purpose: A high-end, scannable "leave-behind" sheet focused specifically on our Motor Vehicle Case Management Services. • Format: Single-sided or double-sided 8.5" x 11" flyer/sell sheet (Layout choice left to designer's discretion for optimal whitespace and impact). • Key Content Blocks to Include (Placeholder text is acceptable for draft): • Header: Company branding and a compelling title (e.g., Navigating Recovery: Expert Motor Vehicle Case Management). • The Company Advantage: A brief section highlighting our professional, meticulous approach to managing complex auto injury cases. • Core Services Bullet Points: * Coordination of medical care, rehabilitation, and specialized therapies. • Attendant care assessments and documentation. • Seamless communication between medical providers, legal teams, and insurance adjusters. • Comprehensive discharge planning and home modifications tracking. • Call to Action (CTA): Prominent placement at the bottom directing readers to contact us for referrals or consultations. • Design Style: Utilize clean grids, professional iconography for service bullets, and excellent typographical hierarchy to ensure it can be scanned in under 30 seconds. Deliverable 3: Branded Sample Case Notes Document (Refinement) • Purpose: A polished, professional document used to showcase a "mock case" to attorneys and adjusters, demonstrating the exceptional detail, clarity, and quality of clients clinical documentation. • Format: Multi-page 8.5" x 11" document template (Word or editable PDF format requested for final delivery). • Project Scope: We will provide an existing, real-world sample case notes document to use as a structural baseline. Your role is not to write content, but to completely overhaul the visual design, typography, and layout to transform it into a marketing-professional, highly branded corporate asset. • Design Requirements: • Header/Footer: Professional, unified running header featuring a clean variant of the logo. Footer should include page numbering ‬and a standard confidentiality notice. • Client Information Block: Convert the existing top-page data into a beautifully structured, clean table or grid to display fields such as Client Name, Date of Injury, Claim Number, Case Manager Name, and Report Date. • Body Layout: Upgrade the typography with distinct, professional styling for headings (e.g., Clinical Assessment, Care Coordination Activities, Progress Summary, Recommended Action Plan) to ensure maximum readability. • Visual Elements: Utilize subtle brand colors for section dividers, callout boxes, or table headers to turn standard clinical notes into an executive-level report.
mcp_workflow_v1: asset_add_file (logo + website brand refs) -> search_design (Express one-pager / folder template) -> fill_text (service overview copy) -> document_merge_data_layout / document_render_layout (case-notes template, client supplies baseline) -> document_convert_pdf (PDF/X print-ready) -> asset_preview_file

desc: We are looking for a direct-response Meta ads designer to create 3 static retargeting ad creatives for TeenTalk, a parenting support system for parents of teens. These are not generic social media posts or cold ads. They are bridge creatives for a video-qualified audience — designed to move warm parents from recognition into our story-driven landing page. The audience has already watched 50%+ of our video ads. The goal is to move warm parents from recognition into action and bring them to the landing page with the right expectation. The creative should make the right parent feel: “This is about me, my teen, my pattern at home — and there may be a real way forward.” Target audience: - English-speaking mothers - Parents of a teen / preteen - Emotionally tired, relationship-driven, and aware there is a real problem at home - They do not want random parenting advice anymore - They are looking for a real system, not one more tip Final deliverables: - 3 static Meta ad creative concepts - Each concept delivered in 3 formats: 1) 9:16 — 1080×1920 — Stories / Reels 2) 4:5 — 1080×1350 — Feed 3) 1:1 — 1080×1080 — Square / flexible placements - Total: 9 exported final image files - Editable source files included if possible: Canva, Figma, Photoshop or Illustrator - Commercial-use / royalty-free visuals only - Clean, readable, mobile-first design - Small TeenTalk logo / purple round stamp in the top corner of each creative - 1 reasonable revision round included - If the final designs do not follow the approved brief, those issues should be corrected Design direction: - Clean, emotional, modern, readable - Serious, warm, credible — not cheesy - Use real emotional mother/teen imagery - Avoid overly staged “perfect family” images - Avoid childish design - Avoid generic therapy-clinic style - Avoid aggressive clickbait - Main text must be readable on mobile - Do not overload with too much text - Strong visual hierarchy: emotional visual, headline, short supporting line, CTA block, small TeenTalk mark Brand direction: Use a clean, premium, emotionally intelligent style. TeenTalk brand colors include soft teal / turquoise, purple, and warm yellow/gold CTA accents. The design should feel connected to a modern parent-support product, not like a generic parenting quote post. The full creative copy and detailed direction for all 3 ads will be provided after hiring. Budget: Up to $120 fixed price for 3 static creative concepts delivered in 3 formats each. To apply, please send: 1. 2–3 examples of Meta / Facebook / Instagram static ad creatives you designed 2. Confirmation that you can deliver 9:16, 4:5 and 1:1 versions 3. title: Photo Sorter / Lightroom Assistant
budget: Hourly: $3.00 - $5.00 Intermediate Est. time: 1 to 3 months, Less than 30 hrs/we
desc: **Photo Culling & Player Sorting Assistant (Sports Photography)** I'm looking for a detail-oriented assistant to help process large volumes of soccer photography. For each match, you will receive a Lightroom Catalog containing several thousand photos. The photos have already been imported and organized. Facial recognition has also been run inside Lightroom to help identify players. Your responsibilities will be: * Review player face clusters and assign them to the correct player. * Group as many valid photos as possible for each player. * Include photos where the player is clearly identifiable, even if: * the face is partially visible, * the player is viewed from the side, * the image is slightly soft but still usable. * Exclude photos that are: * extremely blurry, * completely out of focus, * accidental shots, * unusable for delivery. * Perform a first quality cull to remove weak images while maximizing the number of usable photos per player. * Ensure that no player is missed whenever possible. * Flag uncertain photos rather than guessing. **What you will NOT do (initially):** * No photo editing. * No color correction. * No retouching. * No Lightroom preset work. * No client communication. For now, your role is focused strictly on photo selection and player organization. However, this is intended to be a long-term position. If you consistently produce high-quality work and demonstrate reliability, additional responsibilities and hours may be offered over time. This could include photo editing, Lightroom workflow management, and other parts of the post-production process. **Success looks like:** * Every player has their own collection. * Very few photos are assigned to the wrong player. * The maximum number of usable photos are kept. * Unusable photos are removed. * The catalog is clean, organized, and ready for final review. **Requirements** * Experience with Adobe Lightroom Classic. * Ability to work with large photo catalogs (3,000–10,000+ photos). * Strong attention to detail. * Ability to follow consistent rules. * Sports photography experience is a plus but not required. **Additional Information** * Clear training and SOPs will be provided. * This role is process-driven and does not require advanced editing skills. * This is a repeat position. During the soccer season, multiple matches are processed every week. * I'm looking for someone who can grow with the business as we continue to automate and scale our workflow.

title: Premium Book Cover Designer for Disruptive Anti-Self-Help Book (Amazon KDP & eBook)
budget: Fixed price Intermediate Est. budget: $350.00
desc: I'm looking for a top-tier book cover designer for Stop Fixing Yourself! Why fixing yourself keeps the problem alive. Disruptive anti-self-help book challenging modern therapy clichés, the wellness industry, and toxic positivity. Croatian edition is selling out in physical bookstores. American edition launches on Amazon 2026. Strategic context (read first) This book belongs on the shelf next to Mark Manson, Ryan Holiday, Robert Greene, Cal Newport. It does not belong near Brené Brown, Atomic Habits, or Mel Robbins. The cover must communicate authority and seriousness, not relief or transformation. My reader has already read everything, tried every method, and is still stuck. The cover should make that reader feel seen, not promised to. Critical differentiation The Croatian edition uses a black cover with broken red chains. The American edition cannot copy this. Broken chains have become a cliché in the Amazon anxiety category and would position the book inside the genre it's critiquing. Push the chain metaphor harder and stranger, or replace it entirely with a stronger concept. If you go with chains, they cannot look like any current anxiety book on Amazon. If you go without, typography and composition must carry the full weight. Visual direction Minimalist, bold, premium, slightly dark. Deep blacks, dark greys, high-contrast typography. References in spirit: Holiday, Manson, Greene, Newport. Typography-driven covers usually outperform illustration-driven ones in this category. Strong typographic treatment on dark background with one striking element (or none). Strictly avoid No lotus flowers, sunrises, meditating people, or mountain silhouettes. No smoke, magic wands, mysterious eyes, or hypnosis-as-magic-show imagery. No motivational stock. No bright pastels. No generic broken-chain illustrations that look like every other anxiety book. Filter element on the front cover Include a small but visible line on the front, positioned subtly (above or below title, or as a small badge/stamp): No techniques. No exercises. No protocols. This is a deliberate filter that tells the wrong reader to put the book down. Small enough not to compete with the title, visible at Amazon thumbnail size. Typography Modern impactful sans-serif or elegant modern serif. Author name is Timoteo Crnković with Croatian diacritics. Font must natively support č, š, ć, ž without distortion or manual kerning. Non-negotiable. The Croatian rendering is also a deliberate signal of European authorship in a market saturated with generic American self-help, so it stays. References Three covers I want this to live next to: Mark Manson Everything Is Fucked, Ryan Holiday The Obstacle Is the Way, Cal Newport Deep Work. Croatian edition for reference only, do not replicate: https://prestaniraditinasebi.com https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1Keovs434aTAhTvvM57Cbpq9pwnadL29N Audience Adults 28 to 55 in US, UK, Canada, Australia. Have read multiple self-help books. Familiar with CBT, mindfulness, breathwork, somatic experiencing. Frustrated with the genre but still buying. Not looking for another method. Looking for a different conversation. Deliverables Kindle eBook cover, high-res JPG to current KDP specs. Amazon KDP paperback full cover (front, spine, back) to exact KDP bleed specs. Page count provided once layout is final. Include space for KDP barcode/ISBN, publisher logo (Mental Reset d.o.o.), and one to two QR codes. Back cover must accommodate ~150 to 200 words of body copy, one pull quote, brief author bio, and optional author photo. Copy provided separately. Fully layered source files (PSD or AI), fonts embedded or clearly listed. Three to five high-quality 3D book mockups for marketing, social media, and stopfixingyourself.com. How to apply Start your proposal with the word RESET. Portfolio must specifically highlight dark, minimalist, or premium typography-driven covers. Prioritize examples in non-fiction, philosophy, business, or psychology. Generic portfolios will not be considered.
mcp_workflow_v1: font_recommend (modern sans/serif that natively supports č š ć ž) -> search_design (Express book-cover template, dark minimalist typographic) -> fill_text (title, filter line 'No techniques. No exercises. No protocols.', author 'Timoteo Crnković') -> change_background_color (deep black) -> document_convert_pdf (KDP full cover + eBook JPG) -> asset_preview_file

title: Sports Branding Designer for Logo Refinement
budget: Fixed price Intermediate Est. budget: $80.00
desc: We're looking for a talented Sports Branding Designer to refine and modernize an existing logo concept. This project focuses on enhancing the current design rather than a complete overhaul. Your expertise will help create a production-ready logo that embodies our brand's spirit. If you have a passion for sports branding and a keen eye for detail, we want to hear from you! Please include your portfolio showcasing relevant work in sports logos. What This Is I have an existing logo concept for Georgia Referee — a soccer referee organization operating under US Soccer — that I need taken to a professional, production-ready standard. This is not a vectorization-only job. I am hiring you for your design expertise to refine, polish, and where needed improve the current concept while preserving its core identity. The attached PNG is the reference design. Use it as the creative foundation, not a pixel-perfect spec. The Logo — What Works, What Needs Attention The overall concept is strong and should be preserved: a shield shape, the whistle and crossed referee flags as the central image, "GEORGIA REFEREE" typography, the Georgia peach at the base, and a red/white/blue stripe composition with a silver border. These are locked elements compositionally — do not remove or replace them. Beyond that, I am giving you genuine creative latitude to make this look great. Specific areas that need attention: Flags The crossed flags behind the whistle are the weakest element. The checkered pattern is inconsistent and the rendering feels rough. Please redraw these cleanly. The flag style should read clearly as referee/linesman flags — keep the checkered pattern, but execute it with precision and make the flags feel dynamic and intentional rather than incidental. Lower Composition The area between the whistle/flags and the peach at the bottom is cluttered. The diagonal stripe lines in that zone are competing with the central imagery rather than supporting it. Please find a cleaner solution that gives the peach room to breathe and creates a clear visual hierarchy from the whistle down to the peach. You have latitude here — if that means simplifying the stripe treatment, adjusting angles, or restructuring that section of the shield interior, propose it. Overall Rendering Quality The whistle and shield border have gradient and bevel work that is functional but not polished. Bring these up to a professional standard. The style should feel modern and sporty — think athletic badge, not clip art. Clean gradients, consistent light source, sharp edges where appropriate. Typography The "GEORGIA REFEREE" lettering works well overall. Minor refinements to tracking, weight, or the red stripe framing of "REFEREE" are fine if they improve the result — but do not change the font character or layout significantly without showing me first. Color The current palette is red, white, blue, and silver — a patriotic register that fits the US Soccer context. I am open to moderate color refinement: adjusted shades, better contrast ratios, a more considered palette that still reads as red/white/blue without being a literal flag reproduction. What I am not open to: a full recolor, removing the silver border treatment, or introducing colors that take it outside the patriotic/sporty register. As part of delivery, please document the final approved palette in Pantone, CMYK, RGB, and HEX. We do not have existing specs — you are establishing the standard. Compositional Changes You may propose compositional adjustments — whistle angle, stripe geometry, element spacing — if they improve the overall result. However, please do not implement significant compositional changes without showing me a concept first. A quick sketch or rough mockup for anything structural is fine and preferred over a surprise in the final file. While we have a strong existing concept, we are specifically looking for a designer's expertise. We welcome thoughtful improvements to geometry, spacing, line work, color palette, visual hierarchy, and overall execution. The goal is not strict recreation, but rather a refined, modern, production-ready version that preserves the identity and spirit of the current logo.

title: Professional Media Kit Design for Speaker, Podcast Host, Author & Founder of LivingYosh.com
desc: PROJECT TITLE Professional Media Kit Design for Speaker, Podcast Host, Author & Founder of LivingYosh.com PROJECT DESCRIPTION I am looking for an experienced graphic designer to create a premium, professional media kit that I can confidently send to podcast hosts, publishers, schools, synagogues, event organizers, journalists, and media outlets. The final product should feel modern, inspiring, trustworthy, and professionally branded. It should resemble a high-end speaker or author media kit rather than a basic brochure. ABOUT ME My name is Joshua Markell. I am the founder of LivingYosh.com, a platform focused on faith, growth, purpose, meaningful discussions, inspiring stories, Torah values, and kindness. I am also: • A podcast host and guest • A public speaker • An aspiring author currently developing a memoir • An advocate for personal growth, resilience, faith, and community My personal story includes overcoming significant adversity, rebuilding after major life challenges, and using those experiences to help others grow stronger. MEDIA KIT GOALS The media kit should position me as: • Founder of LivingYosh.com • Speaker • Podcast Host • Podcast Guest • Author / Memoir Project DESIGN STYLE • Clean and modern • Professional and premium • Israel-inspired blue and gold color palette • Strong visual storytelling • Easy to read on desktop and mobile • Suitable for publishers, media, schools, and podcast hosts CORE THEMES Faith • Growth • Purpose Prayer • Torah • Kindness Real Stories • Real People • Real Growth DELIVERABLES 1. Professional Media Kit PDF (6–8 pages) 2. One-Page Speaker Sheet 3. One-Page Podcast Guest Sheet 4. Editable Canva File 5. Print-Ready PDF Version MEDIA KIT SECTIONS • Cover Page • Founder Story • About LivingYosh.com • Speaking Topics • Podcast Host & Guest Information • Memoir / Book Project • Community Impact • Contact Information ASSETS PROVIDED • Professional headshots • Podcast photos • LivingYosh branding materials • Logo • Website information • Social media information IMPORTANT Please include examples of previous media kits, speaker kits, author kits, or branding projects you have designed. Budget-conscious proposals are appreciated, but quality and professionalism are the highest priorities.

title: Quick Turnaround Invite
budget: Fixed price Intermediate Est. budget: $200.00
desc: I need a talented graphic designer to create a polished, professional invitation for a SEAL PAC fundraising event I am hosting in Mount Pleasant, South Carolina. This is a quick-turn project with a final product needed within 24 hours. I will provide the prior event invitation as a visual reference, but this needs to be updated for the new event details, sponsorship levels, venue, and RSVP/payment flow. The previous invite used a patriotic SEAL PAC theme, highlighted the host, event location, special guests, sponsorship levels, and RSVP instructions. The final design should feel patriotic, high-end, clean, and appropriate for a congressional/political fundraising event. It should be suitable for email, text message sharing, social media, and printing. Event Details: Event: SEAL PAC Fundraiser Host: Gavin McCulley Date: July 7, 2026 Time: 12:00 PM to 2:00 PM Location: Tavern & Table Shem Creek, Mount Pleasant, South Carolina Event Description: Join us for a private lunch in support of SEAL PAC, dedicated to electing conservative veterans to Congress. Guests will have the opportunity to meet and spend time with the Congressman, discuss the year ahead, and support SEAL PAC’s mission. Sponsorship Levels: $1,000 Minimum Sponsorship Includes: Lunch Photograph with the Congressman Opportunity to speak with the Congressman about the year ahead SEAL PAC souvenir, such as a hat, shirt, or other commemorative item $5,000 Sponsorship Limited to 5 people Includes everything from the $1,000 level, plus: Private dinner with the Congressman that evening at Hall’s Chophouse Dinner included RSVP / Registration: Please include space for an Eventbrite registration link or QR code. We will provide the link once created. Deliverables Needed: One polished invitation design in PDF format One high-resolution PNG or JPG for texting and social media Editable source file, preferably Canva, Adobe Illustrator, Photoshop, or InDesign Version with QR code or placeholder for Eventbrite link Final files delivered within 24 hours Design Direction: Use the attached previous SEAL PAC invite as a reference for tone and general style, but improve the layout and make it feel more refined and modern. Patriotic colors are appropriate, but the final design should not feel cluttered. It should be clean, premium, and easy to read. Ideal Candidate: Experience designing political, nonprofit, military, veteran, or fundraising event materials Able to work quickly and communicate clearly Strong layout and typography skills Can deliver a professional final product within 24 hours
mcp_workflow_v1: search_design (Express patriotic fundraiser/event invitation template) -> fill_text (event, host, date/time, venue, sponsorship tiers, RSVP) -> asset_add_file (prior invite reference + Eventbrite QR placeholder) -> asset_preview_file -> document_convert_pdf (PDF) ; image_crop_and_resize (high-res PNG/JPG for text/social)

title: Vector Tracing of Owl Logo
budget: Fixed price Intermediate Est. budget: $100.00
desc: I need a clean, manual vector tracing of my existing owl and compass logo to create a perfect SVG file. The task is urgent and requires a production-ready output. The ideal candidate should have experience in vector tracing and logo design, ensuring a high-quality, scalable result. Requirements: 100% faithful to the reference (owl shape, eyes, compass ring, needle/beak, cardinal points, colors) Clean vector paths with good node structure (no messy auto-trace artifacts) Fully editable / layered SVG (owl group + compass group separate if possible) Optimized for web + print (scalable, small file size) Transparent background Deliver: Master SVG file 4096x4096 PNG Monochrome versions (navy only + white only) Small icon versions (512px, 256px, 128px, 64px) Timeline: today (within the next 8 hours). $100 bonus 200 total - if delivered with high quality within the next 8 hours. Please reply with: Your experience with logo vectorization Estimated turnaround (confirm you can do it today) will share files then

title: Vector logo recreation
budget: Fixed price Intermediate Est. budget: $40.00
desc: I have an existing logo I need cleaned up and recreated as production-ready vector files for apparel printing (Printify) and general brand use. My current logo is attached. I don't want it redesigned — just faithfully recreated as clean vectors. Recreate faithfully — preserve exactly: The brushed open-ring (enso) above the wordmark — the ring must stay OPEN in the center, with its hand-drawn brush texture. Do not fill it into a solid circle. The wordmark "HUMAN FRĒQ" in all caps with wide letter-spacing, matching the original. The macron (the bar) over the E in FRĒQ — must be clearly visible and correct. I need TWO versions of the logo: Version A: ring + "HUMAN FRĒQ" only (no web address) Version B: ring + "HUMAN FRĒQ" with "humanfreq.com" added underneath the wordmark Deliverables (for BOTH versions): Vector files: AI, EPS, SVG, and PDF (true outlined vectors, transparent background) Three color versions of each: copper #C09030, cream #EDE5D4, and near-black #3A2C1C Also provide a simplified mark (ring + "FRĒQ" only) for small uses like tags and caps High-res transparent PNGs of every version, at least 4000px wide, 300 DPI Please confirm you can deliver all of this, and include your price, turnaround time, and number of revisions. Thank you!

title: Convert 5 Images to Editable Vectors
desc: I have five highly detailed AI-generated images that my local printer says must be converted to Adobe Illustrator vectors so the colors can be tweaked without losing any of the intricate shading or line work. I personally need to be able to jump back in later and change both the background and the main subjects’ colors, so every layer or object has to remain fully editable. Here’s the scope: • Take the five supplied images and rebuild or trace them in Illustrator so they remain true to the original complexity. • Deliver each file as an .ai (layered, unlocked, colour-editable) and also export a matching print-ready PDF. If you like, you can include a clean PNG as a courtesy, but the AI/PDF is the priority. • Keep the artwork CMYK safe for printing. No embedded rasters that will blur when scaled. • Turnaround is strictly five hours from the moment I award; I’m on a tight schedule today. • My budget is locked at $15 per design ($75 total) and I’m unable to stretch it, so please accept the project only if that works for you. If you can meet those points and hit the five-hour window, I’ll be genuinely grateful and ready to start right away.

title: Adobe InDesign Proposal & Statement of Qualifications (SOQ) Template System for Engineering Firm
desc: Salem Engineering Group, Inc. is seeking an experienced Adobe InDesign designer to create a professional proposal and Statement of Qualifications (SOQ) template system for engineering, environmental, geotechnical, drilling, materials testing, and public agency projects. This is NOT a request for a single proposal template. We are seeking a complete, reusable proposal system that can be maintained internally by our staff after Adobe InDesign training. The completed system should allow our staff to quickly create professional proposals by replacing project-specific information, staff resumes, project experience sheets, references, and photographs while maintaining a consistent company brand and appearance. SCOPE OF WORK The selected designer shall create: • Proposal Cover Page Template • Cover Letter Template • Automatic Table of Contents Template • Section Divider Pages • Standard Content Pages • Staff Resume Templates • Project Experience Templates • Organizational Chart Templates • Appendix Cover Pages • Fee Proposal Cover Pages • Landscape Page Templates • Photo Showcase Pages • Brand Style Guide The system must be fully editable and designed for long-term internal use. DESIGN OBJECTIVES The final design should be: • Professional • Modern • Clean • Technical • Easy to Read • Appropriate for Government Agencies • Appropriate for Public Works and Engineering Proposals The design should not resemble a marketing brochure or sales flyer. COVER PAGE CONCEPTS Prior to development of the final template system, the designer shall provide three (3) different cover page concepts for review. One concept will be selected and used as the basis for the final design. FILES PROVIDED BY SALEM We will provide: • Company Logo • Existing Proposals and SOQs • Project Photographs • Staff Photos • Existing Resumes • Existing Project Experience Sheets • Company Information DELIVERABLES Designer shall provide: • Adobe InDesign (.INDD) Files • IDML Files • Packaged Project Files • Master Pages • Paragraph Styles • Character Styles • Table Styles • Linked Graphics Folder • Font List • Brand Style Guide REQUIRED WITH BID SUBMISSION To be considered, bidders MUST provide the following with their proposal: • At least three (3) examples of proposal, SOQ, RFQ, RFP, annual report, or similar multi-page document designs completed by the bidder. • At least one (1) example related to Engineering, Architecture, Environmental, Construction, Consulting, Government, or Professional Services industries. • Examples must include both cover pages and interior pages. • A brief description of the bidder's role in each project. • Confirmation that the work was created in Adobe InDesign. Preference will be given to bidders with demonstrated experience creating proposal systems, SOQs, RFQs, RFP responses, and qualification packages for Engineering, Environmental, Architecture, Construction, or Consulting firms. Bids submitted without relevant work examples may not be considered. PROJECT GOAL The final product should provide Salem Engineering Group with a professional, reusable proposal system that can be used for future RFQs, RFPs, SOQs, marketing packages, and qualification statements while maintaining a consistent company brand and appearance. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY AND ORIGINAL WORK REQUIREMENTS All custom layouts, templates, graphics, design elements, and branding materials created specifically for Salem Engineering Group under this project shall become the exclusive property of Salem Engineering Group, Inc. upon final payment. The final design system shall be created specifically for Salem Engineering Group and shall not be based upon or substantially derived from commercially available proposal templates, stock proposal packages, or previously developed client templates without prior disclosure and written approval from Salem Engineering Group. Any stock photography, fonts, graphics, icons, or third-party assets requiring separate licensing must be disclosed prior to use. The designer shall identify any third-party assets incorporated into the final design and provide licensing information where applicable. No AI-generated logos, branding elements, company identity materials, or stock proposal templates shall be used as the primary basis of the final design. The designer shall not reuse, resell, license, distribute, or repurpose the completed Salem Engineering Group proposal system, templates, layouts, or custom design elements for any other client without written permission from Salem Engineering Group. The designer shall disclose any use of artificial intelligence tools in the design process. Any AI-generated content used must be fully editable, commercially usable, and approved by Salem Engineering Group prior to inclusion in the final deliverables. The designer shall provide: • Native Adobe InDesign (.INDD) files • IDML files • Packaged project files • Linked graphics • Style libraries • Font list • All custom graphic elements created for this project Upon final payment, Salem Engineering Group shall receive full ownership and unrestricted usage rights to all custom designs, layouts, templates, graphics, and project deliverables created under this agreement. The final proposal system must be fully editable by Salem Engineering Group staff using Adobe InDesign without requiring assistance from the designer after project completion. To confirm that you have read the entire project description, please begin your proposal with the phrase: "I have read the complete project description."

title: Catalog designer needed — B2B electrical/elevator parts distributor (100+ pages)
budget: Hourly Expert Est. time: Less than 1 month, Less than 30 hrs/week
desc: About us Beta Electrical Supply is an active wholesale distributor of electrical and elevator parts. We sell to contractors and service companies. We have a logo and a product list. We need an experienced catalog designer to rebuild our product catalog from the ground up — better than what we have, better than our competitors. Who reads this catalog Our typical buyer is an elevator service technician, maintenance supervisor, or electrical contractor. They reach for our catalog when they need to spec a replacement part fast, prepare a quote, or verify Beta is a real supplier before placing a first order. They read it both in print and as a PDF on their phone in the field. Scope Full catalog redesign — roughly 100 pages, final count to be decided in kickoff. Open to a tighter, more curated version (40-60 pages) if that better suits our buyer. Front cover, back cover, intro page, Table of Contents, section dividers, product pages, reference tables (weight/amperage chart), closing page with contact info. Consistent visual system across product sections — coherent header treatment, table style, image presentation. Tables must read well in print AND on phone screens. Cover tagline: propose 3 short tagline options for us to pick from. Use our existing logo (a blue hexagon with up/down arrows). Minor cleanup is fine; full logo redesign is not in scope. Final deliverables Print-ready PDF (CMYK, bleeds, crop marks) AND web-optimized PDF (RGB, compressed). Editable source file (InDesign INDD with packaged links and fonts). We need to update prices and add products ourselves later — no PDF-only handoff. Organized handoff folder: fonts list, color codes, photo library, README with file structure. What we provide Our existing logo files. Structured product list: codes, descriptions, specs, dimensions, category groupings (Excel or CSV). Company contact info, addresses, official email. Product photography (see Photo Source below). Competitive reference (after NDA signed): we'll share a competitor's published catalog as a piece we want to look distinctly different from. You may study it for context; you may not copy any element (layout, photography, tagline, color, table style). We will not send our own current internal draft — it contains material we cannot redistribute. We'll provide clean product data separately. Photo source — to be decided in kickoff We have ~200 SKUs across ~20 categories. We'll pick one primary path: A) We shoot in-house (white sweep, phone, basic light kit). B) Manufacturer-supplied photography (3M, James Monroe, Brugg, Smartrise, etc.) — you clean and standardize. C) We send what we have, you handle background removal and color correction. Quote your per-image rate. All photography must be material we legally own or have licensed. Design direction Reference: McMaster-Carr is the gold standard for B2B parts catalogs — dense, utilitarian, easy to find a part fast. Clean type hierarchy, restrained color, spec tables over marketing copy. Feel: clean, professional, modern industrial. Not flashy. Confidence through restraint. Print quality: the printed catalog should feel like it cost real money to produce. Hard requirements Originality. You attest in writing that no layouts, photography, taglines, copy, or graphics will be lifted from any competitor catalog or third-party source without license. Reverse-image-search spot checks may be run at each milestone. IP assignment. All deliverables assigned to Beta Electrical Supply on final payment. Standard work-for-hire. Mutual NDA before competitive reference is shared. Editable source. Final InDesign package with packaged links and fonts. We test-open it on our machine before final payment. Timeline & payment Target completion: 2 weeks max. Milestone-based payment via Upwork escrow (25% at kickoff / 25% at first 5 pages approved / 25% at full draft PDF / 25% on final delivery). We commit to same-day or next-day feedback turnaround at every review gate. How to apply Include in your proposal: Two or three examples of B2B / industrial / technical catalog work — at least one of 30+ pages. Portfolios of only logos, websites, or consumer marketing will not be considered. How you would approach this project (one paragraph). Your software stack. Your rate and rough timeline. Any questions you have about the scope. Generic templated proposals will be ignored

title: Cinematic Teaser Video Creation
budget: Fixed price Intermediate Est. budget: $50.00
desc: Upwork Project Brief Cinematic Teaser Video – Major New Protect Group Australia Launch About Us Protect Group Australia & New Zealand is a rapidly growing protection technology company operating nationally across Australia and New Zealand. We specialise in advanced surface protection systems, coatings, asset preservation solutions, and aftermarket technologies across automotive, caravan, marine, industrial and fleet sectors. We are preparing to launch a major new division and product category into the Australian market and require a world-class teaser video designed to generate excitement, curiosity and anticipation. Project Objective Create a premium cinematic teaser video that announces a major upcoming launch without revealing exactly what the product, brand or opportunity is. The goal is to make viewers stop scrolling and think: "What is Protect Group bringing to Australia?" The video should create speculation, discussion and excitement while positioning Protect Group as a serious, fast-growing company making significant moves within the industry. This is not a sales advertisement. This is a brand-building teaser campaign. Video Requirements Length 45–60 seconds Formats Required 9:16 Vertical (Primary) 16:9 Landscape 1:1 Square Quality Ultra-premium Commercial-grade Cinematic Broadcast quality Creative Direction The video should feel similar to: Tesla product launch teasers Porsche launch films Apple keynote launch videos Formula 1 promotional content High-end luxury automotive advertisements Global OEM manufacturer campaigns The feeling we want: Powerful Mysterious Premium Industrial Technical Confident Sophisticated Future-focused What we do NOT want: Canva-style editing Generic social media videos Cheap transitions Overuse of effects Basic PowerPoint-style animations Generic detailing industry marketing Important Instructions DO NOT reveal: Product name Brand name Manufacturer name Packaging Logos Product labels Website screenshots Any direct references that identify the launch We want viewers guessing. Assets Available We can provide: Protect Group Content Vehicle footage Caravan footage Workshop footage Warehouse footage Team footage Product application footage Existing social media content Additional Content Editor may use: Stock footage AI-generated footage Motion graphics Cinematic overlays Premium transitions Story Flow Scene 1 – Growth Show rapid growth. Warehouse. Operations. Products moving. People working. Business activity. Text examples: 4 Months Ago A Vision Became Reality Australia Took Notice Scene 2 – Momentum Fast-paced cinematic cuts. Vehicles. Caravans. Protection technology. Business growth. National coverage. Text examples: 400+ Protections Sold 150+ Applications Completed Growing Nationally Building Something Bigger Scene 3 – Innovation Shift into more technical and futuristic visuals. Close-up shots. Precision work. Technology. Engineering. Advanced materials. Reflections. Light. Industrial environments. Text examples: Innovation Never Stops The Next Chapter Begins Scene 4 – Global Scale Introduce visuals suggesting international growth and manufacturing. Examples: Shipping containers Manufacturing facilities Engineers Global logistics Production environments Premium vehicles Text examples: Global Technology Exclusive Opportunity Coming To Australia Scene 5 – The Tease Music builds dramatically. Quick cuts. Close-up detail shots. Protection. Precision. Technology. Engineering. No product reveal. Text examples: COMING SOON AUSTRALIA 2026 Final Scene Protect Group logo reveal. Text: PROTECT GROUP AUSTRALIA & NEW ZEALAND Enjoy your journey, you're protected for life. Voiceover (Optional) If included, voiceover should sound like a premium movie trailer. Deep. Authoritative. Confident. Example tone: Four months ago, a vision became reality. What started as an idea became a movement. Hundreds of customers. Thousands of protected kilometres. A growing national presence. But this is only the beginning. The next chapter is coming. Bigger. Smarter. More advanced. Australia, get ready. Music Direction Music is critical. Desired style: Hans Zimmer-inspired Formula 1 launch videos Luxury automotive campaigns Technology reveal events Requirements: Cinematic tension Deep bass Modern percussion Powerful build-up Strong ending Avoid: Generic corporate music Upbeat marketing tracks Cheap stock music Motion Graphics Typography should be: Clean Modern Minimalist Premium Think Apple or Tesla presentations. No gimmicky effects. No cheesy animations. No clutter. Deliverables Final master file Vertical version (9:16) Landscape version (16:9) Square version (1:1) Editable project files Licensed music files used All source assets created for the project Success Criteria

title: Digital Ad Designer (Contractor)
budget: Hourly: $50.00 - $69.00 Expert Est. time: More than 6 months, Hours to be determ
desc: This is not a graphic design role. We produce ads at scale, to spec, for brands that measure every dollar. If your portfolio is brand decks, social posts, and print work, this isn't the right fit. If you've spent years inside ad accounts, know why a messy UGC creative beats a polished studio shot, and can deliver 15 correctly sized, correctly weighted assets from a brief without a check-in, read on. The Role We are a performance-driven agency managing paid media across Meta, Google, TikTok, and programmatic for a portfolio of brands. We're building out our production roster and need contract designers who can execute fast, think like a media buyer, and deliver assets that are built to convert, not just to impress in a PDF. You'll be briefed by our Digital Project Manager. Briefs include platform specs, mood boards, copy, and performance guidance on what actually works for that brand. Your job is to execute and communicate when something in the brief won't work. What You'll Produce Static and animated ad assets across Meta (Feed, Stories, Reels), Google Display, TikTok, programmatic, and email, delivered in all required size variations, typically 8 to 15 per concept. Platform-ready files: correct dimensions, correct file format, within spec on weight, every time, without reminders. Motion assets where required: GIFs, short-form video, HTML5, including native-feeling, lo-fi UGC-style executions that don't look like agency ads. Rapid iterations: when feedback comes, you turn it fast. Revision cycles are tight and we expect responsiveness during active projects. Performance-informed work: if you believe a direction will underperform, you say so and offer an alternative. We want your expertise, not just your execution. Hard Requirements | Non-Negotiable Your application will not move forward without these: 6+ years designing digital ad creative specifically for paid media, not general graphic design, not social content. A portfolio showing ad creative across multiple platforms and multiple brands, with visible evidence that you understand platform format differences. You can name current file size limits for Meta feed images, Google Display, and TikTok video without looking them up. You know the difference between a 1:1, 4:5, 9:16, and 1.91:1, and you know which placements they serve. Figma and/or Adobe Creative Suite (Photoshop, Illustrator, After Effects), proficiency, not familiarity. Ability to work from a brief to final delivery with minimal hand-holding. We will not chase you for updates or explain what a safe zone is. Do Not Apply If: Your ad experience is limited to organic social. Your portfolio only shows brand identity or print work. You need platform specs explained to you. You require detailed direction beyond a well-written brief. What Will Make You Stand Out Prior work at a performance marketing agency or in-house at a DTC brand where creative was measured against ROAS, CTR, or CPA. Strong instincts for visual hierarchy that drives action: you design for the scroll stop and the click, not just the screenshot. Experience producing lo-fi, UGC-style creative that feels native, and knowing when that's the right call versus a polished execution. Motion and animation skills for short-form video and GIFs, especially thumb-stopping first frames that function within TikTok and Reels native environments. Familiarity with AI creative tools and the judgment to know when they help and when they don't. Experience helping build or improve a production workflow, not just working inside one. How We Work Project volume varies by month. We scope each engagement upfront and confirm availability before kicking off. We won't drop a 40-asset batch on you without warning. Strong performers get first right of refusal on new projects and will see volume grow as we do. We're building a roster we can rely on, not a revolving door of one-off contractors. Assets are delivered in organized, clearly named folders with all required sizes. We review against specs and performance standards before anything goes to a client. If something needs a revision, we'll tell you exactly what and why.

title: Fun Real Estate Video Editing
desc: I shoot 15–20-minute YouTube videos that showcase local property tours, market tips, and community highlights. The footage itself is solid; what I need is an editor who can turn it into a lively, binge-worthy experience that keeps viewers watching to the very last second. Style & pacing • Overall tone: fun and engaging rather than stiff or strictly “corporate.” • Quick cuts, light jump-cuts, and tasteful zooms to keep energy high. • Colour correction and audio levelling so everything looks polished and sounds clear. Text overlays The only mandatory graphic element is strategic call-to-action text (e.g., “Book a showing,” “Subscribe for weekly tours,” “Download the market report”). These should appear at natural breaks, match my brand colours, and animate in smoothly without blocking key visuals. Optional creative touches I’m open to your ideas—subtle sound beds, whip-pan transitions, or light motion graphics—provided they support the upbeat real-estate vibe and don’t overwhelm the main content. Deliverables • One ready-to-upload 1080p (or higher) MP4 for each episode • Separate short teaser (≤60 sec) repurposed from the same timeline would be a bonus • Project file (Premiere Pro, Final Cut, or DaVinci Resolve) so I can tweak if needed • Turnaround: within 3 business days of receiving raw footage If you love editing content that blends property expertise with an entertaining touch, let’s talk and see if our styles click.

The editable project file (Premiere Pro, Final Cut, or DaVinci Resolve—whichever you use) so I can tweak titles later if needed. Acceptance checklist - Smooth color grading across all sources. - Seamless audio transitions that match on-screen action. - No watermarking; all third-party assets cleared for personal use. - Delivered via a shared drive link within the agreed timeline. If this sounds like your style, let me know your preferred editing software and roughly how many days you’d need once you receive the files. I’m ready to send the footage as soon as we agree.

title: Graphics Designer
budget: Fixed price Expert Est. budget: $15.00
desc: I am a digital agency owner looking to work with either a reliable freelance graphic designer or a design agency for ongoing client projects. I need someone who understands professional design delivery, clear communication, deadlines, and the importance of providing editable/source files with every project. This position will be for ongoing client design work, so I am looking for someone who is accountable, friendly, professional, organized, and ready to grow with my agency. Requirements You must be able to provide: * Vector/source files for every design project when applicable * Final files organized in a ZIP folder * Two mockups included with every logo design * Mockups included for other services when applicable * Print-ready files when needed * Transparent PNGs when needed * Editable files such as AI, EPS, SVG, PDF, PSD, or Affinity files * Professional communication and timely updates * Work completed in the CST timezone Communication Communication is very important to me. I use Slack for internal communication and email for client-related work. If you cannot complete a task, meet a deadline, or need more time, I need to know ahead of time — not right before the due date. I need someone who communicates clearly, gives updates, asks questions when needed, and is honest about timelines. NDA, Confidentiality, White-Label Work, and Ownership You must be comfortable signing an NDA and working as a white-label contractor for my agency. All work completed for my agency must remain private and confidential. This includes client names, business information, files, designs, concepts, login details, brand assets, project details, communication, and anything else related to the project. You understand that you are working under my agency, not directly with my clients. You will not contact, message, email, follow, tag, or communicate with my clients unless I give written permission. You also understand that my agency is the face of the project. All work must be delivered to me first, and I will handle all communication, revisions, approvals, and final delivery with the client. Once the project is completed and paid for, you transfer full ownership and usage rights of the final design files to me/my agency. I then have the right to transfer ownership and usage rights to my client. You may not reuse, resell, claim ownership of, recreate, publish, post, watermark, or display the work in your portfolio, website, social media, marketplace, or promotional material unless I give written permission. You may not use my clients’ names, brands, logos, designs, or project details as examples of your work unless I approve it in writing. All source files, vector files, editable files, exports, mockups, and final ZIP folders must be delivered directly to me/my agency. Payment Rates All services include editable/source/vector files when applicable. Final files must be delivered in an organized ZIP folder. Logos / Branding * Simple logo design: $15 * Logo revision/edit: $10–$15 * Logo vector cleanup: $15 * Logo color variations: $10 * Logo export package: included * Two logo mockups: included * Final ZIP folder with all files: included Every logo project must include: * Main logo files * Logo with text folder * Logo without text folder * AI file * EPS file * SVG file * PDF file * PNG file * Transparent PNG file * JPG file * Two mockups Business Cards * Single-sided business card: $15 * Double-sided business card: $30 * Business card revision/edit: included unless it becomes a major redesign * Print-ready PDF setup: included * Editable/source file: included * Final ZIP folder: included Every business card project must include: * Editable/source file * Front design * Back design if double-sided * Print-ready PDF * PNG preview * JPG preview * PDF proof * Mockup when applicable Flyers / Posters * Basic flyer: $15 * Detailed flyer: $25–$35 * Double-sided flyer: $30–$45 * Event PDF * PNG preview * JPG preview * PDF proof * Social media version when included * Mockup when applicable Social Media Graphics * Instagram/Facebook post: $10–$15 * Story graphic: $10 * Carousel post: $20–$35 * Facebook cover: $15–$25 * YouTube thumbnail: $10–$20 * TikTok/Reel cover: $10 * Editable/source file: included * Final ZIP folder: included Every social media graphic project must include: * Editable/source file * PNG file * JPG file * Correct size for the platform * Transparent PNG when needed * Mockup when applicable Website Graphics * Website banner/hero image: $20–$35 * Section background graphic: $15–$25 * Website icon set: $20–$40 * Promo banner: $15–$25 * Editable/source file: included * Final ZIP folder: included Every website graphic project must include: * Editable/source file * PNG file * JPG file * Transparent PNG when needed * Web-optimized version * Correct size requested Apparel / Merch Graphics * T-shirt design: $20–$40 * Hoodie design: $25–$45 * Neck label design: $15 * Patch / woven label design: $15–$25 * Product mockup: $10–$20 each * Print/vector/source files: included * Final ZIP folder: included Every apparel or merch project must include: * Editable/source file * Vector file when applicable * PNG file * Transparent PNG file * Print-ready file * Mockup file * Placement preview when needed Brand Add-ons * Mini brand guide: $25–$50 * Color palette only: $10 * Font pairing: $10 * Simple pattern design: $20–$40 * Icon-only logo mark: $15–$25 * Editable/source files: included * Final ZIP folder: included File Delivery Requirement for All Services All design services must be delivered in a clean, organized ZIP folder. Each final ZIP folder must include the following when applicable: * Editable/source file * Vector file * PDF file * PNG file * Transparent PNG file * JPG file * Print-ready PDF for print projects * Mockup files when applicable * Fonts used or font names/links * Any approved assets used in the design Accepted editable/source file types include: * AI * EPS * SVG * PDF * PSD * Affinity Designer file * Canva editable link only if approved before starting No project should be sent as only a screenshot, flattened PNG, or JPG. Source/editable files are required for every service when applicable. Revision Policy Basic revisions are included as long as they are reasonable and stay within the original project direction. I do not charge separately for small revisions such as text changes, color adjustments, spacing changes, layout tweaks, or minor edits. However, if the revision is close to starting over, changes the entire direction, or becomes a completely new concept, then it may be treated as a new project or require an additional fee that is agreed on before continuing. title: Social Media Coordinator
budget: Hourly: $6.00 - $7.00 Expert Est. time: More than 6 months, Less than 30 hrs/wee
desc: Remote | Part-Time Contract | Social Media & Community Engagement About Grocapitus Investments Grocapitus Investments is a commercial real estate investment firm focused on multifamily, townhome, mixed-use, and apartment development projects. We help investors generate passive rental income without becoming landlords through our syndication investment opportunities. Our marketing team supports investor education, thought leadership, events, social media, and brand growth across the Grocapitus ecosystem. We are looking for a highly organized and detail-oriented Social Media Coordinator to help manage content publishing, social media scheduling, audience engagement, AI-assisted content creation, and campaign coordination. This role is ideal for someone who enjoys working with creative teams, building online communities, staying organized, and ensuring content is published consistently and on time. Our Core Values Commitments AI-FIRST — Data-Driven Creativity & Accountability We actively use AI and data-driven decision making to improve marketing execution, systems, workflows, reporting, creativity, and accountability. Balance — Hard Work & Fun in Equal Measure We work hard, move quickly, support one another, and believe great work environments should also be enjoyable and energizing. Nimble, Energetic & Resilient in Everything We Do We adapt quickly, stay resourceful under pressure, maintain momentum through challenges, and continuously look for better ways to improve and grow. Position Overview The Social Media Coordinator will support the planning, scheduling, publishing, and engagement activities across the Grocapitus social media channels. This role combines social media coordination, audience engagement, AI-assisted content creation, content scheduling, and campaign coordination. The right candidate is highly organized, detail-oriented, proactive, tech-savvy, and comfortable managing multiple projects simultaneously in a fast-paced remote environment. This person will work closely with the Graphic Designer, Marketing Coordinator, and VP of Marketing & Operations to ensure campaigns, promotions, events, and marketing initiatives stay on track and launch successfully. This is a remote, part-time contract position. Key Responsibilities Social Media Coordination & Publishing Create, schedule, and publish social media content across LinkedIn, Facebook, Instagram, YouTube, and other platforms. Maintain and manage the social media content calendar. Coordinate campaign schedules, approvals, and publishing timelines. Work closely with the Graphic Designer to ensure assets are completed and delivered on time. Coordinate with the Marketing Coordinator and VP of Marketing & Operations to ensure campaigns remain aligned with priorities. Review scheduled content for formatting, links, branding consistency, and quality control. Organize and maintain content libraries and marketing assets. Monitor social media channels daily and respond to comments, messages, and direct messages (DMs) in a professional, timely, and brand-consistent manner. Engage with followers, prospects, event attendees, investors, and community members to help strengthen relationships and increase engagement. Escalate inquiries, leads, customer service issues, or opportunities to the appropriate team members when necessary. Track content performance and assist with basic reporting. AI-Assisted Content Creation Use ChatGPT and company-provided AI power strings to generate social media captions, post copy, headlines, promotional messaging, and content variations. Edit and refine AI-generated content to ensure it aligns with brand voice and quality standards. Repurpose long-form content into social media posts and promotional assets. Continuously improve AI-assisted workflows that help the marketing team execute more efficiently. Marketing Coordination & Campaign Support Coordinate campaign timelines, deliverables, approvals, and publishing schedules. title: Social Media Creative

desc: Hi, I’m looking to create a full set of professional ad creatives for my website, TheRewardsMaven.com. The ads should look clean, trustworthy, premium, and professional — not flashy, spammy, or overly aggressive. Important: Please do not mention any specific banks or card issuers, such as Chase, Amex, Capital One, Citi, etc. The ads should only promote The Rewards Maven as a travel rewards / points and miles guidance website. I need ad creatives for multiple platforms, and each platform should be designed in the format that is most standard and professional for that platform: 1. X / Twitter sponsored posts Please create ad creatives suitable for temporary sponsored posts or pinned posts on popular X accounts. The standard format should be short post copy plus a clean static image, and optionally a short MP4 version. Needed: * 1600×900 landscape image * 1080×1080 square image * Optional 8–12 second MP4 video version * 2–3 short caption options for the post Style: clean, text-light, professional, good for finance/travel audiences. 2. Instagram Stories / Facebook Stories / WhatsApp Status / Snapchat These platforms are mostly vertical and visual, so I need story-style ads. Needed: * 1080×1920 vertical static image * 1080×1920 vertical MP4 video, 10–15 seconds * Keep text away from the top and bottom safe areas so it does not get covered by profile icons, buttons, or link stickers * Clear headline, brand logo, short benefit, and call-to-action Style: premium vertical story ad, simple movement if video, not too much text. 3. Instagram Feed / Facebook Feed For feed placements, I need clean social ad graphics. Needed: * 1080×1080 square image * 1080×1350 portrait image * Optional 10–15 second MP4 version * 2–3 caption options Style: professional social media ad, polished but not too salesy. 4. TikTok / Reels-style vertical video For TikTok and Reels, the standard is short vertical video, not a regular banner. I need a native-looking video ad that feels like content, not a cheap flyer. Needed: * 1080×1920 vertical MP4 * 15–30 seconds * Strong hook in the first 2 seconds * On-screen captions * Simple CTA at the end * Can include website screenshots, travel visuals, points/miles visuals, and clean motion graphics Style: native short-form video, modern, trustworthy, and clear. 5. Telegram / WhatsApp groups / community posts These placements usually work best with simple text plus an image, not overly designed ads. Needed: * 1080×1080 square image * 1080×1920 vertical image for status/channel use * Short caption text * Longer caption text Style: should feel like a useful recommendation, not a loud advertisement. 6. Website banner ads / private website placements I also need standard banner ads that can be placed directly on blogs, news sites, travel sites, tech sites, and other private websites. Needed standard sizes: * 300×250 medium rectangle * 728×90 leaderboard * 970×250 billboard * 300×600 half-page/sidebar * 320×50 mobile banner * 320×100 large mobile banner * 160×600 skyscraper Please provide static PNG/JPG versions. If you can also create subtle animated HTML5 or MP4/GIF versions, please mention that separately. The animation should be clean and professional, not flashing or spammy. 7. Google Display Ads / Google Ads graphics I also need graphics that can be used for Google Display or responsive display ads. Needed: * 1200×628 landscape image * 1200×1200 square image * 1200×1500 vertical image * 1200×300 logo/banner style image if useful * Clean logo version if needed Style: simple, high-quality display ad creative with strong branding and minimal text. General creative direction: * Brand: The Rewards Maven * Website: TheRewardsMaven.com * Topic: travel rewards, points, miles, travel credit card guidance, rewards news * Tone: expert, trustworthy, premium, clear * Avoid: hype, flashing GIFs, fake urgency, bank/card issuer names, “guaranteed approval,” or anything that sounds misleading * Preferred formats: static images and short MP4 videos. title: YouTube Thumbnail Graphic Designer
budget: Hourly: $20.00 - $40.00 Intermediate Est. time: 1 to 3 months, Less than 30 hrs/
desc: We are seeking a creative designer to craft engaging YouTube thumbnail graphics. The ideal candidate will have experience in graphic design and video editing, with a strong portfolio showcasing their work. Responsibilities include designing visually appealing thumbnails that capture viewer attention and align with our brand's aesthetic. If you have a passion for design and a keen eye for detail, we would love to hear from you! Lincoln Square Media is looking for a specialized YouTube Thumbnail Designer who understands that in the world of political media, a thumbnail is more than just a picture—it’s the headline. We need a visual strategist who combines journalism graphics with high-impact design to maximize Click-Through Rate (CTR) and audience engagement. This is a freelance position focusing on our long-form podcasts, deep-dive reporting, and short-form video content. You will be responsible for creating the "packaging" that stops the scroll and compels viewers to click in a highly competitive news environment. ________________________________________ Key Responsibilities • High-Impact Design: Create custom 16:9 thumbnails for long-form political podcasts, endcards and 9:16 assets for Shorts/Reels that stand out on a crowded homepage. • A/B Testing & Optimization: Design multiple variations (2-3 per video) for YouTube’s "Test & Compare" feature to identify which visual cues drive the highest watch-time share. • Journalistic Visuals: Use high-quality photo manipulation, typography, and infographics to represent complex political topics accurately yet provocatively. • YouTube Psychology: Apply color theory, facial emphasis, and "pattern-breaking" layouts to increase CTR and capture the "politically homeless" audience. • Channel Branding: Design consistent, high-energy endcards, channel banners, and platform-specific overlays. • Short-Form Support (Plus): While primarily a design role, the ability to assist with short-form video clipping or basic motion graphics is a major plus. ________________________________________ About You You Bring: • Proven Track Record: Experience designing thumbnails for high-traffic YouTube channels, preferably in the news, politics, or commentary space. • Technical Mastery: Expert-level skills in Adobe Photoshop. You know how to make subjects "pop," color grade for drama, and use bold, readable typography. • News Judgment: An understanding of the political landscape. You know which faces and symbols are relevant and how to frame a "hook" without being clickbaity or misleading. • Data-Driven Mindset: You aren't just an artist; you care about the analytics. You design with the intent to beat the previous video’s CTR. • Efficiency: Ability to work on a freelance basis with quick turnaround times (24–48 hours) to keep pace with the news cycle. • (not required, but preferred) Short-form vertical video editing skills/digital content creation ________________________________________ Apply Interested? We’d love to hear from you. Please submit your resume, examples of thumbnails created, YouTube links where your work can be seen, and a short cover note about what you’d be excited to contribute to Lincoln Square Media. If applying as a freelancer, please indicate your hourly rate Note: We are specifically looking for examples of thumbnails that demonstrate: • High contrast and bold, readable text. • Expressive facial imagery or compelling political graphics. • Clear examples of how you’ve packaged a "story" in a single frame. Want To Move Ahead of the Pack? Show us an example or a two of a You Tube Thumbnail that you’d create for a streaming show seen on our website at lincolnsquare.media, as well as examples of any short-form, vertical video editing (if applicable). ________________________________________ What We Do Lincoln Square Media provides relentless reporting and thoughtful analysis in defense of democracy. We are a creative, mission-driven media organization producing pro-democracy articles, columns, podcasts, short-form video series, and digital shows that reach millions across platforms. Our work combines compelling storytelling, smart editorial vision, and fast-paced production to inform, inspire, and engage audiences. We are a rapidly growing media organization dedicated to cutting through misinformation, holding power accountable, and empowering Americans with the facts needed to take civic action.
