title: Graphic Designer | Corporate Design
budget: Hourly: $15.00 - $40.00 Intermediate Est. time: 3 to 6 months, Less than 30 hrs/
desc: Flow Keeping is looking for a reliable freelance graphic designer to support the creation of branded marketing assets for our funnel, Google Ads campaigns, social media content, landing pages, and ongoing digital marketing needs. We are building out a full marketing system and need someone who can help us create high-quality visuals that are clean, modern, polished, and aligned with our brand. This role is ideal for a designer who understands how to design for marketing performance, not just aesthetics. The right person should be able to take direction, work from existing brand assets, follow creative briefs, and produce designs that feel consistent across multiple channels. We need someone who can move quickly, communicate clearly, and help us get a strong volume of useful creative without exceeding the budget. This will begin as a part-time role with a maximum of 20 hours per week. If the working relationship is successful, this may become an ongoing role for future campaigns, content launches, ad creative, and brand development. About Flow Keeping Flow Keeping helps customers better understand and manage their water usage through smart, practical, and easy-to-understand solutions. Our brand needs to feel trustworthy, modern, helpful, clean, and professional. The design style should support a business that is building credibility, educating customers, and converting visitors through paid ads, organic social content, landing pages, and funnel-based marketing assets. Main Responsibilities Create polished, professional marketing graphics for Flow Keeping. Design assets that support our funnel, Google Ads campaigns, and social media content. Work from creative briefs, written content, examples, and brand direction. Create multiple design variations when needed for testing. Keep all creative consistent with the Flow Keeping brand. Organize final files clearly so they can be used by our marketing team. Deliver editable source files when requested. Communicate clearly about timelines, design questions, and project needs. Use time efficiently and stay within the approved weekly hour limit. Ideal Candidate The ideal designer should have experience with: Graphic design for digital marketing Social media design Google Ads or paid ad creative Landing page graphics Brand-consistent design systems Canva, Adobe Illustrator, Photoshop, Figma, or similar tools Creating templates for ongoing marketing use Designing for conversions, not only visual appeal Working with small businesses or startups with limited budgets We are especially interested in designers who can create clean, modern, trustworthy visuals without making the brand feel too generic, overly corporate, or cluttered. Design Style We Are Looking For The creative should feel: Clean Modern Trustworthy Professional Easy to understand Conversion-focused Organized Approachable Consistent with the Flow Keeping brand Weekly Workflow We expect the designer to work up to 20 hours per week. Each week may include a combination of: Social media graphics Ad creative Funnel graphics Revisions to existing designs Template development Campaign-specific creative Brand asset cleanup We will prioritize the most important assets first so the budget is used wisely. Budget and Time Management Because our marketing budget is already stretched, we need a designer who is comfortable working within clear priorities and time limits. We are not looking for someone who spends excessive time on unnecessary design exploration. We need someone who can understand the direction, create strong work efficiently, and help us produce the assets needed to support active marketing campaigns. To keep the project manageable, we will: Limit work to 20 hours per week Assign tasks by priority Start with the most urgent funnel, ad, and social assets Approve larger batches of work before additional hours are used Focus on reusable templates wherever possible Avoid unnecessary redesigns unless they directly improve performance or brand consistency Required Skills Graphic Design Digital Marketing Design Social Media Design Ad Creative Design Canva Adobe Photoshop Adobe Illustrator Figma Brand Design Marketing Funnel Design Layout Design Template Design Not Required but a plus Skills Experience designing Google Ads creative Experience with conversion-focused design Experience with startup or small business marketing Experience creating design systems or reusable templates Experience designing for home services, utilities, SaaS, smart technology, or consumer products Basic understanding of marketing funnels Basic understanding of paid ad creative testing Deliverables Depending on the project, deliverables may include: PNG files JPG files PDF files Editable Canva files Adobe source files Figma files Organized folders of completed creative Multiple size variations for ad placements Social media post sizes Google Ads display sizes Reusable branded templates Application Requirements Please include the following in your proposal: A short introduction explaining why you are a good fit for this role. A link to your portfolio. Examples of social media graphics, ad creative, or funnel-related design work. Your hourly rate. The design tools you prefer to use. Your availability each week. A brief explanation of how you manage design work efficiently when a client has a limited budget.
mcp_workflow_v1: search_design (Express social-post + display-ad templates) -> fill_text (Flow Keeping copy/CTA) -> asset_add_file (Flow Keeping logo + product imagery) -> image_crop_and_resize (Google Display + social placement sizes) -> asset_preview_file

title: Monthly Basis Video Editing

Expect a variety of short- to mid-length pieces—some aimed at social channels, others formatted for more traditional uses. title: Pastel Social Media Photo Edits

Please return the images in both high-resolution JPG (for archiving) and optimized PNG or JPG sized for social platforms. Consistent naming and layered PSDs are a plus if you work in Photoshop. mcp_workflow_v1: asset_add_file (ingest the 11–30 images) -> image_apply_auto_tone (baseline) -> image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation (pull saturation down toward soft pastel) -> image_adjust_hsl + image_adjust_color_temperature (gentle pastel palette shift, skin tones kept believable) -> image_adjust_light_portions (airy/lifted pastel feel) -> image_list_presets/image_apply_preset (lock the pastel look as a preset and apply across the whole batch for a cohesive feed) -> image_remove_background on the files with distracting backgrounds -> image_fill_area with a chosen neutral pastel colour (clean backdrop) -> image_crop_and_resize (per-platform sizes: IG 1080x1350/1080x1080, Pinterest 1000x1500, FB) -> asset_preview_file

Requirements: You must have extensive experience in high-end wedding photography and know how to handle tricky lighting scenarios (harsh Colorado sun, green grass color casts, dark/mixed-light receptions). Please provide a link to your portfolio. Sending at least one full wedding gallery you have recently edited would be a huge plus. Next Steps: If your style aligns with mine, I will hire you for a PAID test run. I will send you a Lightroom catalog with anchor images and you will edit the remainder of the wedding. If it goes well, I would love to make it an ongoing position.

The ideal candidate will have experience in graphic and print design, with a keen eye for detail and creativity. The project requires intermediate proficiency and is expected to be completed in less than a month.

title: Wedding Template Designer — Long-Term Work Inside Our Online Editor
desc: About Us: WeddingTemplates.com is a digital wedding stationery brand offering customizable, downloadable templates for DIY brides. Couples customize our designs in our free browser-based editor, then download and print at home (or share via link, in the case of online invites). We've built a catalog of 80+ templates across three categories and are now expanding. We need a talented designer who can build beautiful, on-brand templates directly inside our editor. What You'll Design: You'll create wedding templates across three product categories: 1. Invitations & Stationery — invitation suites, save-the-dates, RSVP cards, details cards, thank-you cards 2. Ceremony & Reception Essentials — menus, programs, place cards, table numbers, seating charts, signage, favor tags 3. Online Wedding Invitations — mobile-first digital invites designed for sharing via link, text, or email Each template line needs to be delivered across 5 distinct aesthetic styles: Minimalist, Floral, Boho, Classic, and Rustic. Range matters — you should be able to credibly deliver all five without them feeling like the same designer's hand. Where You'll Work — Important: All design work happens inside our proprietary browser-based editor. You won't be sending us PSD or AI files. You'll build templates natively in our editor so they're immediately publishable and customer-ready. We'll provide full onboarding. If you've used Canva, Adobe Express, or Figma, the workflow will feel familiar — the constraint is that final output must live in our editor, not be exported from external tools. What We're Looking For: - 3+ years of design experience, ideally in wedding, event, or stationery design - Strong typography and layout skills — wedding stationery lives or dies by typographic detail - Demonstrated range across styles (especially minimalist + boho + classic, which require very different sensibilities) - Comfort working in browser-based design tools (Canva, Figma, Adobe Express, etc.) - Print-savvy: understanding of bleed, margins, standard paper sizes (5x7, 4x6, 4x9), and home-printer-friendly design - Self-directed — you can take a brief, deliver clean work, and flag questions early - Reliable communication (responses within ~24 hours) - Nice to Have: - Wedding industry background (planner, florist, photographer, stationer) - Familiarity with the Etsy/Canva wedding template aesthetic landscape - Experience designing for print-at-home customers (Avery cardstock formats, perforated paper, etc.) - Project Details: - Type: Long-term, ongoing - Start: ASAP - How to Apply: Please include: 1. Portfolio link with at least 3 wedding, event, or stationery samples 2. One sample (or pointer to existing work) per style if possible — even quick examples — so we can see your range across Minimalist, Floral, Boho, Classic, and Rustic 3. A short note answering: which of the 5 styles is your strongest, and which would stretch you? 4. Confirmation that you're comfortable doing all design work inside a custom browser-based editor (no Photoshop/Illustrator final delivery) Applications without a portfolio will be skipped. Looking forward to seeing your work.

title: Wedding favor logo
budget: Fixed price Entry Level Est. budget: $60.00

I'll provide a picture of our invitations so it can be referenced. Our colors are pastels and I'm just looking for something simple with some flowers.
mcp_workflow_v1: asset_add_file (invitation reference image) -> search_design (Express label/sticker template with floral motifs) -> fill_text (names, date, 'thank you' / favor text) -> asset_preview_file -> document_convert_pdf (print-ready sticker sheet)

title: Wedding photo culling + editing in Lightroom
budget: Hourly Intermediate Est. time: More than 6 months, Hours to be determined
desc: Hello! I’m an established wedding photographer currently looking for an experienced wedding photo editor to join my team on a long-term basis as I am at capacity! (see example of my work attached) I shoot around 30-40 weddings per year and am looking for someone with a strong eye for detail, experience in Lightroom skills, and preferably experience specifically within wedding photography editing + culling My workflow: • I photograph the wedding day • I run the full gallery through Aftershoot AI using my editing profile • I create sneak peeks (typically 80–150 images in both colour and black & white) • I send you the full Lightroom catalogue for culling + final editing What I’m looking for from you: • Cull the gallery manually (not with AI, human eye only), selecting the strongest images with careful attention to blinking, facial expressions, storytelling moments, composition, and artistic choices • Refine and finesse the edits across the gallery to ensure consistency with the sneak peeks, including exposure, white balance, cropping, straightening, some spot removal and noise reduction adjustments where needed • Create black & white versions for approximately 20% of the final gallery using my preset • Return the completed Lightroom catalogue ready for client delivery The gallery will already have a strong AI base edit applied through Aftershoot or Imagen, so I’m specifically looking for someone to do the final polish and bring the gallery to completion with a human eye and attention to detail. Requirements: • Previous experience editing wedding galleries • Strong Adobe Lightroom experience • Excellent attention to detail and consistency • Reliable communication and turnaround times • A genuine supporter of queer/same-sex weddings, as I photograph many LGBTQIA+ couples and want someone aligned with those values If this sounds like a good fit, I’d love to hear from you, along with examples of your work, experience, pricing, and turnaround times. Thank you and looking forward to hearing from you!

title: Engaging Motion Graphics for Schugle
desc:
Schugle (pronounced "shoe" + the "gle" from "Google") is a German web product that helps teachers create, formulate, structure and revise Foerderplaene (individual pupil support plans) with AI — while protecting sensitive pupil data by replacing real names with anonymised placeholders before anything reaches the AI. The current website is factual and dry; the client wants a coherent visual campaign that makes the idea attractive, memorable and emotionally clear while staying calm, credible, professional and suitable for schools. The mandated narrative arc is "Messy notes -> structured thoughts -> protected placeholders -> AI-assisted formulation -> clear Foerderplan," with the tilted Schugle "g" icon as a recurring motif. The client explicitly forbids generic education clichés (happy teachers, smiling children, chalkboards, graduation caps, cartoon kids, generic "school app" imagery).

Produce THREE related videos that read as one campaign: (1) a 10-second clip that also works as a SILENT website hero/background loop (must read with no voiceover and be visually calm), (2) a 30-second promotional/social/reel version, and (3) a 2-minute explainer that reuses material from the first two. German on-screen text is required and MUST remain editable — the client may swap German copy or add German voiceover later, so text must live as editable layers, never flattened into the only final file. Required German on-screen strings (faithful to the product): "Verstreute Notizen" (scattered notes), "Struktur statt Stapel" (structure instead of piles), "Echte Namen bleiben geschützt" (real names stay protected) with an anonymised placeholder like "[Schüler:in A]", "KI-gestützte Formulierung" (AI-assisted formulation), and "Klarer Förderplan. Weniger Schreibarbeit." (clear support plan, less paperwork).

Constraints and deliverables: master the campaign at 1080p, 30fps, H.264 MP4. Deliver each of the three videos in three aspect ratios — 16:9 (1920×1080, website hero), 9:16 (1080×1920, reel/Stories) and 1:1 (1080×1080, feed). Because the in-connector reframe tool (video_resize) is SUBJECT-AWARE, not text-aware, the German on-screen text is NOT baked into a single master before reframing; instead the brand motion is reframed first and the German text is placed PER-RATIO afterward (as an editable text overlay) so it stays legible and in-frame in every ratio. Provide a clean NO-TEXT version of the 10s hero loop (silent, for the website background) — generated from a separate text-free brand animation, not by stripping text off the text version. Deliver the licensed Stock music bed as a separate WAV stem (the brief: "separate audio files/tracks for music"); loudness normalization to roughly -16 LUFS is performed as an out-of-band local audio step (ffmpeg loudnorm) because no connector tool normalizes loudness — the 30s and 2-min carry the music, the 10s hero stays silent. If captions are shown, supply a German SRT (authored locally from the campaign copy, then uploaded). Finally deliver a license manifest (CSV/MD) listing every font, Stock asset (with license id), Express template id and music track used, with license terms. The visual style must echo the calm, trustworthy German-school tone of the named reference (foerder-fokus.de) without copying it, and must be grounded in how the real product actually flows (see the supplied reference tutorial clip).

title: Cinematic Tribute Video for Guru Vandana cum Birthday Event (Shri Anil Tolia Ji, 250 guests)
desc:
Context: A family is organizing a "Guru Vandana" (guru-reverence) cum birthday celebration for Shri Anil Tolia Ji, to be attended by roughly 250 friends, family members, children, and guests. The centerpiece of the function is an emotional life-journey tribute video projected on a large LED screen/projector during the event. The piece must feel emotional but positive, respectful and heartwarming, inspirational, elegant and premium — and must AVOID loud flashy editing, overly dramatic effects, fast social-media-style cutting, and distracting animations. The desired register is "cinematic family tribute / elegant slideshow-documentary," with smooth transitions, slow zoom/pan, and tasteful typography.

The ask: Using the supplied raw materials — chronological photo sets (childhood, college & friendship, career, marriage & family, recent, and Guru-Vandana moments), several raw home/event video clips, and a noisy live on-stage tribute speech recorded at rehearsal — produce a 5-8 minute Full-HD (1920×1080, MP4) tribute that follows the client's exact 8-section flow: (1) Opening Tribute, (2) Childhood Journey, (3) College & Friendship Days, (4) Career & Achievements, (5) Family Journey, (6) Guru Vandana Section, (7) Birthday Celebration, (8) Closing Message. The agent must mine the raw clips for the most emotional, usable moments, auto-cut a tight cinematic "memory sizzle" from the chosen clip segments to seed the montage, clean the noisy on-stage tribute speech into a warm, broadcast-clean narration track (or a warm/mature English VO with optional Hindi/Gujarati blessing lines), score the whole thing with soft, licensed, royalty-free piano/light-orchestral/flute music that blends under — and never overpowers — the narration, and lay elegant section titles and a closing thank-you over the cut.

Constraints & deliverables: Output 1 = the stage-screen MASTER at exactly 1920×1080 (16:9), H.264 MP4, 5-8 minutes, clean enough for large-screen projection, music bed mixed roughly 14-18 dB under the VO. Output 2 = a short (≤60s) vertical 1080×1920 (9:16) social/WhatsApp share cut derived from the master's best moments (subject-aware reframed), same audio treatment. Output 3 = the cinematic sizzle (intermediate, reused in the master, kept addressable). Output 4 = the cleaned narration WAV. Output 5 = an asset-credits / music-and-source sheet (PDF) listing every licensed Stock track and the source clips used, as the client explicitly requires "music and asset details used." All music must be royalty-free/licensed; narration must be warm, encouraging, emotional, mature and respectful; nothing flashy. The family expects one or two revision rounds, so the agent must keep the intermediate assets (cleaned VO, licensed score, the sizzle, the master) individually addressable for re-edits.

EXECUTION-MODE NOTE (per CONNECTOR_CAPABILITIES_v2.1): this brief is PRODUCT-doable, not autonomous-headless. The Express title track (search_design, fill_text) needs a user gallery pick = [W]; the video/audio connector tools (media_summarize, video_create_quick_cut, media_enhance_speech, video_resize) are async-widget = [A] and notify in the product; photo grading, asset search, static layout rendering, and inline preview are headless = [C]; loudness measurement and the final mux/trim are local ffmpeg = [L]. Capability discipline matters: video_resize is SAME-LENGTH (the ≤60s trim is done by video_create_quick_cut first), media_summarize gives transcripts/scene summaries only (no dB loudness — that is measured locally), document_render_layout renders only STATIC 1920×1080 frames (all motion/timing is in the ffmpeg mux), media_enhance_speech is run only on the VOICE (the licensed music stem is delivered as-is), and the credits page is authored with document_render_layout (document_convert_pdf cannot merge/author pages).

title: Create Professional Shopify App Store Demo Video — Avatar Intro + Website/App Walkthrough
desc:
DisputeDesk is a Shopify app that helps ecommerce merchants manage chargebacks and assemble structured dispute evidence. The client is preparing their Shopify App Store listing and wants to replace a rough, amateur screen recording with a polished product-introduction film. They have provided a long raw screen-capture walkthrough of the app, a separately recorded male business-avatar intro clip, a capture of the marketing homepage, a scratch voiceover take recorded in a non-treated room, a brand kit (logo, colors, fonts), and a licensed corporate music stem. The film must open with the professional, calm, trustworthy avatar presenter introducing the chargeback problem and the product in a B2B-SaaS register, then transition smoothly into the website/homepage section, then into a guided app walkthrough with professional zooms, callouts, UI highlights, clean text overlays, and smooth transitions — guided by the voiceover, never feeling like someone randomly clicking around.

The exact, gradeable constraints from the client: the final master must be 1600 × 900 pixels, 16:9, exported as MP4; it must NOT crop important parts of the website or app UI; on-screen text must be readable; and the audio must be clean and balanced throughout (no room noise, no awkward pauses, consistent loudness between the avatar intro and the walkthrough narration). The visual style is clean, modern, professional and trustworthy — polished but not flashy, no distracting effects — on par with professional B2B software demo videos. The walkthrough must be tightened from the long raw capture into a focused highlight that tracks the narration. The film carries three structural beats: a ~15s avatar intro, a ~15s website/homepage transition, and a ~60s narration-guided app highlight (~90s total).

Production reality per the corrected capability sheet: the video/audio steps (media_summarize, media_enhance_speech, video_create_quick_cut, video_resize) are async-widget [A] tools that run in the Adobe×Claude product and notify via a progress widget — they are not retrievable in an autonomous headless harness. The on-brand text callouts use the Adobe Express interactive [W] track (search_design returns templates headless; fill_text and animate_design need the user to pick a template in the gallery). Final compositing — concatenating the three beats, overlaying the animated alpha callouts, demuxing the avatar audio for enhancement, muxing the two loudness-matched VO tracks and the music stem laid in low, and scaling to exactly 1600×900 with no crop — is a single local ffmpeg [L] step, because the connector has no compositor/mux/audio-extract tool. The music stem is delivered as-is and never passes through media_enhance_speech (a speech-only denoiser, not a music processor). Length changes use video_create_quick_cut; video_resize is used only for same-length subject-aware reframes.

The deliverable set is the 1600×900 MP4 master plus a 9:16 (1080×1920) vertical promo and a 1:1 (1080×1080) square autoplay preview, both reframed subject-aware from the same master so no important UI is cropped, plus inline previews for sign-off. All copy stays consistent with the brand kit; the music bed is subtle and credible, sitting low under the voiceover.

title: Brand Identity & Marketing Asset Package for Growing PC Company
desc:
Tech World PC is a growing custom-computer company (custom gaming PCs, workstations, refurbished systems, computer repair, upgrades and components) that currently sells primarily through eBay and other online marketplaces and wants a professional, consistent brand identity that scales with the business. They have completed the concept phase and selected a logo DIRECTION — a geometric hexagon icon with an integrated 'TW' monogram, clean corporate styling, strong readability at small sizes — but the only artifact they have is rough AI-generated concept artwork from brainstorming. They are explicit that they do NOT want a blind trace of the AI image: they want the concept recovered into clean professional vector form. (Note: the connector set provides image_vectorize for raster->clean-SVG recovery but no bespoke vector path-editing tool; this spec therefore treats the cleaned vectorize output as the connector-feasible 'rebuilt vector' and notes that any further hand path-refinement is an out-of-connector source-file step, so the anti-trace requirement is graded against a clean, non-garbage vector rather than against path editing no tool can do.) The icon must function independently from the wordmark so it can live in watermarks, social profile pictures, product photos, stickers and future packaging. The brand must feel like a professional technology company in the spirit of Intel, Corsair, Lenovo, ASUS and EVGA — Professional, Modern, Trustworthy, Technical, Premium, Scalable — and must AVOID esports branding, mascots, cartoon characters, Twitch-style graphics and aggressive gaming-team logos.

The deliverable is a complete brand package. (1) LOGO SYSTEM: from the supplied concept, produce a clean vector mark and its required lockups — Icon-Only, Light-Background, Dark-Background (white mark on dark canvas), Single-Color (#0B1F3A) — exported as transparent PNGs at 512/1024/2048/4096px plus the editable vector (SVG) and an 800x800 profile crop. (Recolors are produced at render time by re-styling the master SVG fill — NOT by raster overlay — and the dark-BG lockup is a white-mark-on-dark-canvas composite, since that variant is two operations a single overlay cannot do.) (2) BRAND CORE: a concise brand-guide concept covering logo usage, color palette (a primary 'tech blue' #1F6FEB plus a dark slate #0B1F3A and a neutral #E8EDF4, in the Intel/Lenovo family — NOT neon esports color), typography recommendation, spacing/clear-space, and background-usage examples. (3) MARKETING & SOCIAL ASSETS built on Adobe Express templates the user selects from the gallery and filled with the real brand name and taglines (text-only fills), THEN explicitly exported to spec-exact PNGs: an eBay store banner (1200x270), a 'Play the Latest Games' promotional banner (1500x500), a Facebook cover (1640x624), a YouTube banner (2560x1440 with safe area), a business card (1050x600), and a profile-picture crop (800x800). A listing watermark (the Icon-Only mark over a product photo at reduced opacity) is produced by a local layout composite, NOT by generative/solid fill. (4) MOTION: a short animated logo sting (~3-4s, square 1080x1080) — the refined mark placed into a real Express/Canva design and animated with the PRESET fade/zoom reveal the connector supports (no bespoke keyframe 'assembling' build, no esports glitch). (5) PRESENTATION: every render — the refined mark, the colorway, the populated templates, the watermark demo, the brand guide and the sting — assembled into one Firefly concept board the client can review as the 'cohesive brand system' before final source-file handoff.

Hard constraints the agent must respect: the mark must be derived from the SUPPLIED concept (consistent hexagon + TW monogram), not invented from scratch; all logo PNG exports must be on a TRANSPARENT background at the four exact sizes (512/1024/2048/4096); the single-color and dark-background versions must be legible (no color-on-color illegibility); the brand name 'Tech World PC' and exactly the listed taglines must appear correctly spelled across every template via text-only Express fills (layout and chosen corporate font not visually broken) AND must be exported to the exact deliverable dimensions; the palette/styling must read corporate-tech with zero esports/mascot/Twitch cues; the animated sting must use the SAME refined mark; and the final concept board must contain every produced asset so a single deep-link presents the whole system. No step may use a not-available capability (generative fill, AI object/element removal, photo compositing-by-prompt, text-to-image, image->PDF, or video trim-to-timestamp).

title: Brand Identity + Packaging Designer for Premium Men's Skincare/Grooming Brand
desc:
BARDE Grooming (working name, shared only with the awarded freelancer) is launching a premium men's grooming/skincare brand. The flagship is a 50ml post-shave / post-haircut roll-on serum aimed at men prone to razor bumps, ingrown hairs, irritation, and dark marks around the neck and beard line. The brand must read premium, masculine, modern, barber-led and performance-focused — built for both DTC ecommerce and barbershop retail — and must NOT look generic, overly clinical, feminine, or like a cheap private-label product. Visual direction: matte black / deep charcoal packaging with cream, white, silver, or deep-red accents; a minimal-but-bold layout; a strong wordmark; and a social-media-friendly, lifestyle-driven feel (reference point for boldness only, not to copy: 'Sleep or Die').

You are hired for the Full Brand Package. Deliver: a logo/wordmark, color palette, typography system, and a basic brand style guide; the 50ml roll-on sleeve/label design; an outer product box design; a patch pouch design; a routine/instruction card; product mockups and social assets; and print-ready files with dielines plus editable source files. Enriched for this benchmark into a long-horizon pipeline, the engagement also requires: (a) a Firefly moodboard that locks the brand world from the supplied bottle/label concepts, the 6-SKU line reference, and licensed textures; (b) a hero product shot placed on the locked brand-charcoal world via a solid-color backdrop fill and tonal balance; (c) that hero outpainted into a wide ecommerce/social banner and cropped to platform sizes; and (d) the locked label design rolled across the brand's full six-SKU line via an Illustrator Variables data-merge driven by a user-authored template, then collated locally to one print-layout booklet.

Constraints and specs: 50ml roll-on shrink-sleeve/label must carry a 2mm bleed and the supplied dieline, keep critical content >=3mm inside trim (3mm safety margin), and be delivered as print-layout artwork with dielines/bleed retained plus editable source. SKU line = 6 records driven by the provided CSV (variant name, concern, strength, size, accent color). Banner = 2400x1000 px; social crops = 1080x1080 (IG square) and 1080x1920 (story). Routine card = A6, 2-up. Style guide = single sheet with palette (hex + CMYK swatch values), type system, and logo lockups. The same vectorized logo and font system must appear consistently on the label, patch pouch, box, card, and guide — brand consistency is explicitly graded. IMPORTANT CAPABILITY NOTES (grounded to the connector): the hero's distractions are NOT removed by an AI tool (object/generative removal is not available) — the supplied hero is already clean and image_fill_area is used only for a solid-color backdrop fill; the SKU rollout uses a USER-AUTHORED Illustrator data-merge template with real Variables; the 6-page label booklet is assembled with a local PDF step because the connector cannot merge files into one PDF; CMYK is authored as swatch values, not a verified color-engine conversion. Hand off all artifacts in one organized CC folder with previews.

title: B2B Industrial Catalog Designer — Poultry Equipment Pilot + Full Catalog Rollout
desc: TSI Poultry is a multi-branch industrial and poultry-equipment distributor (Louisiana, Oklahoma, Tennessee) building a new catalog for its poultry division. The engagement is two-phase, and this task covers Phase 1, the pilot: the Winching & Suspension Systems catalog — roughly 10-12 pages covering ~75 SKUs in a single product category. The explicit point of Phase 1 is not just a pretty PDF; it is to lock in the design system, typography, and a reusable template that Phase 2 (the full multi-category poultry catalog) will scale from. The client provides everything needed: a rough mockup ('a direction we like, not a brief — genuinely open to your professional take'), a price list with every SKU, description, and cross-reference part number to industry-standard equivalents, a full set of product photography shot in-house on white background (no stock, no reshoots), and brand assets (TSI Poultry logo and color palette). The desired aesthetic is 'industrial precision' — technical-documentation restraint, premium without being precious — for an audience of purchasing managers, integrators, and farm operators who care about specs and part numbers more than decoration but who respect a serious, well-built reference document.\n\nThe deliverables are fixed and specific: a print-ready PDF (8.5\"x11\", correct trim/bleed, CMYK); a web/email PDF (compressed, sRGB, with a hyperlinked table of contents); an editable source template (InDesign preferred); and a short template/style guide so Phase 2 can scale consistently. The honest workflow is a long-horizon data-merge over the ~75-row spec, and it must respect the connector's real execution modes. First, prepare the photos: the in-house white-background shots carry the usual uneven white-point, table-seam, and soft-shadow artifacts, so each of the 75 images is normalized to a uniform 2000x2000 cell (image_crop_and_resize), the hardware is cut cleanly off its imperfect backdrop with image_remove_background, exposure and white-balance are corrected (image_apply_auto_tone, image_adjust_color_temperature), and the transparent subject is flattened onto a true #FFFFFF canvas in a local compositing step (image_fill_area is solid-color-fill-only and cannot composite, so the flatten is done off-connector). The cleaned cells are re-uploaded for stable URNs. Then the document pipeline: the agent's USER-AUTHORED master InDesign template (real <<merge fields>>) is bound against the CSV/JSON via document_merge_data_layout (an authored-template [T] step) to drive every per-product page, pulling each SKU's cleaned-photo URN; an authored Illustrator-Variables template drives the cross-reference and price-per-quantity rider sheets via document_merge_data_vector ([T]). The fixed pages (cover, hyperlinked TOC, divider) are produced with document_render_layout, with CMYK/bleed/crop-marks and the working internal TOC links baked into the render spec (because document_convert_pdf cannot inject color-space, compression, or hyperlinks). Each page-set is exported to PDF with document_convert_pdf in its supported spec->PDF direction; the print PDF is then assembled by concatenating the three page-sets locally (convert_pdf does not merge multiple files), and the compressed sRGB web PDF with its live TOC is derived locally. The style guide is rendered then converted the same render->convert way. Finally the four deliverables — print PDF, web PDF, style guide, and the editable .indd/.ai source — are copied into a clean handoff folder.

title: Bold Monreale Perfume Photography
desc:
Monreale is launching a new unisex fragrance line and needs a campaign image set that feels "bold, modern and instantly appealing to a younger, unisex crowd," built around contrast-heavy lighting, saturated color accents, and confident product placement (per the attached inspiration board and the five flagged reference frames). The work splits into two parts exactly as the client states: (1) clean PRODUCT imagery that shows every faceted edge of the faceted-glass bottle, and (2) LIFESTYLE frames that place the scent in an energetic, real-world context. You are given a single clean studio capture of the Monreale bottle on a plain sweep, a concatenated inspiration/mood sheet (the five flagged references), a brand sheet PDF with the palette, lighting notes and required file types, and a hand model/lifestyle plate with an empty bottle-sized gap. You must license a rights-cleared studio backdrop/prop set from Adobe Stock (standard license, unlimited global use), use image_generative_expand to extend the single bottle capture into a wide 16:9 hero canvas and a vertical 9:16 story canvas (blending toward the licensed backdrop ground), and build the lifestyle frame by extracting the bottle with image_remove_background and compositing it into the empty hand gap with a local layout/compositing step (the connector has no AI compositor, so placement and contact-shadow are done locally with PIL, then the composite is graded by the same connector chain).

The campaign palette is deep ultramarine #1B2A6B as the hero ground, brushed-gold #C9A14A as the accent, and warm-neutral skin tones in the lifestyle frames; the look must read high-contrast with saturated blue/gold accents and crushed-but-clean shadows. The grade is DEFINED ONCE on the product base and then RE-INVOKED IDENTICALLY on every frame group: one preset, one auto-tone, and the exact same exposure / brightness-contrast / highlights / color-temperature / HSL / vibrance offsets, all recorded in monreale_grade_recipe.json and replayed call-for-call so the product and lifestyle images feel shot on one set. Deliver 8 frames total: 3 product-hero frames (the bottle on the licensed backdrop, differentiated by three distinct crops/framings off the graded base - full-bleed centered, tight facet-detail crop, and offset-with-negative-space), 2 wide 16:9 hero banners, 1 vertical 9:16 story frame, and 2 lifestyle frames (bottle composited into the hand plate, and a tighter surface crop of the same composite). Each of the 8 frames ships as BOTH a print-master TIFF and a web-ready JPEG (16 image files total). Before the full run, produce the client's requested "quick preview": a Firefly concept board that pulls the graded first frames together with the inspiration sheet so the client can confirm the lighting direction.

Constraints: web-ready files are sRGB JPEG, long edge 2048 px (except the two fixed-size hero/story webs below), 72 dpi, quality ~85; print-masters are 16-bit TIFF, 300 dpi, color-managed (sRGB/Adobe RGB tagged). The 16:9 wide-hero web JPEGs are exactly 3840x2160 and their print-masters are the same 16:9 ratio scaled to a 3840 px long edge at 16-bit/300dpi (the single, consistent hero master target - there is NO 3508 px target; the earlier A4 figure is removed). The 9:16 story web JPEG is exactly 1080x1920 and its master is the same ratio scaled to a 1920 px long edge at 16-bit/300dpi. All eight frames must share one preset + one auto-tone + the same exposure/contrast/highlights/temperature/HSL/vibrance offsets, and each of those connector calls is made explicitly on each frame group (no "conceptually applied" hand-waving) - consistency is provable from the step list, not eyeballed. Brand accents must survive the grade - the gold accent must not skew green and the ultramarine must not crush to black. The backdrop must be the licensed Stock asset (provide license proof), and rights must be cleared for unlimited global use.

title: Refine Existing Logo into Elegant Spiritual Brand Identity
desc:
Gateway Spiritualism is a spiritual/philosophical project (podcasting, writing, Substack articles, YouTube videos and public-facing content) centered on inner change, emotional honesty, and awakening from self-judgment and endless self-improvement. The client already has an existing logo/artwork concept (attached) but it reads as symbolic cover art, not a true brand identity. They explicitly do NOT want a new concept from scratch and do NOT want any piece of the original reused verbatim — instead, refine and distill the existing imagery into a professional, elegant, timeless logo and brand symbol that works cleanly across Substack, podcast profiles, YouTube, business cards, social media, and website branding. The core symbolism to PRESERVE is: gateway/doorway (must remain central), awakening, awareness/perception, transformation, and inner passage/transition. The eye-as-awareness idea may be used only if abstract, elegant, subtle and integrated into the gateway — never a literal/realistic eye, and never anything occult, psychedelic, conspiracy-oriented, fantasy/New-Age cliché, or 'all-seeing-eye.' The brand must feel thoughtful, emotionally intelligent, spiritually reflective, calm, elegant, minimalist, timeless, and quietly profound — and must NOT feel loud, flashy, corporate, 'life-coach,' mystical-fantasy, overly New Age, or visually busy. Visual style: clean lines, negative space, restrained elegance, symbolic simplicity, modern minimalist spirituality; the mark must stay recognizable and powerful at small sizes.

Deliverables requested by the client: (1) a refined primary logo; (2) a simplified icon/symbol version; (3) typography recommendations for 'Gateway Spiritualism'; (4) versions for light and dark backgrounds. File deliverables: a fully scalable vector master (SVG), transparent PNG exports, horizontal and stacked logo variations, black-and-white versions, a social profile/icon version, a business-card-compatible version, typography/font recommendations, and logo files suitable for both web and print. Ownership: full commercial and proprietary rights to all final approved artwork plus editable source files. The client also asks: avoid stock icons and recycled logo templates; treat the attached artwork as inspiration/source rather than reusing it directly.

The long-horizon, execution-mode-aware version asks the freelancer to take this through one continuous chain. HONEST CAPABILITY POSTURE (per the corrected connector sheet): the original artwork is ingested only as INSPIRATION; a NEW distilled minimalist gateway mark is generated as an input asset (prompted from the brand_voice_notes preserve/forbidden arrays, using the original purely as visual reference), uploaded, and vectorized into a single scalable SVG master — this honors 'refine, do not trace verbatim.' Brand type is recommended and a restrained palette is locked. The refined identity is then APPLIED two ways: (a) on the Adobe Express PRODUCT track [W] — the user picks a business-card template from the search_design gallery, fill_text sets the wordmark + mantra TEXT (text only — it never places the mark), and change_background_color sets the brand palette; (b) via genuine InDesign .indd layout renders [C] — document_render_layout PLACES the vector mark + type + palette to produce the full light/dark + horizontal/stacked + B&W variation sheet PDF and the A4 letterhead lockup (this is where the mark is actually composited, because fill_text cannot place an image). A gentle 'doorway-opening' brand reveal is animated on the Express lockup [W]; a 1:1 reframe is requested via video_resize [A, async] with a local ffmpeg [L] reframe/mux as the retrievable fallback (same length, no trim). Finally everything already produced — original-vs-refined mark, palette, type, applied card, rendered lockups, and the motion intro (as a thumbnail still) — is assembled into one Firefly concept board [C] for client review, with all static finals organized in a CC delivery folder [C].

title: Premium Medical Device Brochure Design - RADIVA Shockwave Therapy System (8-page digital + press)
desc:
Biotech - a manufacturer with 32 years in rehabilitation equipment - is launching RADIVA, a next-generation Solid-State Radial Pressure Wave Therapy (RPWT) system, and needs a premium ~8-page product brochure that positions it against leading medical-device brands (Zimmer, Storz Medical, BTL, Chattanooga). This is explicitly NOT a generic product catalog: the brochure must communicate Innovation, Intelligence, Clinical Confidence and Premium Technology, with a clean, modern, minimal, technology-driven European medical-device aesthetic. RADIVA's selling points must be surfaced as structured spec/feature content: Solid-State Electromagnetic Technology, Soft Shock Technology (patient comfort), the BIOS (Biotech Operating System) running an RTOS on a quad-core 64-bit ARM processor, a 10-inch capacitive touchscreen, a built-in clinical protocol library with user-created and QuickLink protocols, and real-time parameter adjustment during treatment.

The brochure has a fixed 8-page running order: (1) Cover, (2) RPWT Overview, (3) Solid-State Technology, (4) Soft Shock Technology, (5) BIOS Software Platform, (6) Intelligent Clinical Workflow, (7) Real-Time Treatment Control, (8) Closing / Company Experience. The client PROVIDES finished product photography (a clean studio device hero), software screenshots, the company logo, brand rules and detailed content; the freelancer sources any supporting clinical/lifestyle imagery, normalizes and grades all imagery to one cohesive look, produces a knocked-out device cut-out and a full-bleed outpainted cover hero, and lays out the spec tables and feature pages so every interior page is visually consistent. NOTE: because AI object/cable removal and generative fill are not available connector capabilities, the device hero is treated as an already-clean capture rather than a raw shot the connector cleans up; the legitimate connector retouch work is background knock-out and AI canvas-extend (outpaint), not object erasure.

Deliverable framing: the brief's PRIMARY deliverable is the digital brochure (an ~8-page premium digital brochure) plus a print-ready PDF; the print master is therefore added as a deliberate enrichment. Constraints: A4 portrait (210x297 mm) with 3 mm bleed page geometry; a print master at 300 dpi assembled and print-prepped (RGB->CMYK soft-proof + crop-mark overlay) as a LOCAL print-prep finish, since CMYK colorspace conversion and crop-mark stamping are not headless-attested connector capabilities; a second screen-optimized digital PDF at 150 dpi, RGB, no crop marks (live hyperlink injection is out of scope - no tool injects PDF links); a single cohesive image grade (cool, high-key, low-saturation) across both client-shot and Stock-sourced imagery; brand-appropriate European-medical typography; and exactly 8 pages in the order above. Deliverables are the digital PDF and the print-ready PDF, plus a Firefly concept board (image previews only) capturing the supplied->cut-out/outpaint->graded->laid-out provenance for client sign-off.

title: Canva Wedding Website Template Designer — Full Etsy Listing Package (Old Money Style)
desc:
A solo Etsy seller is launching a digital-products shop selling 'Save the Date' wedding-website template kits, and needs a complete, sellable listing package for the first SKU in an 'Old Money / Luxury Wedding' style. The fixed palette is dark burgundy, cream/ivory, and gold accents with lace-detail textures; the overall feel must read high-end and editorial, never basic or generic. The kit centers on a 7-page wedding-website template (Hero/Monogram with couple names + wedding date; Envelope Reveal; Save the Date; Wedding Details with venue/time/dress code; Our Story with photo + timeline; RSVP form; live Countdown Timer) that buyers receive as an editable Canva share link and that must work on mobile. The seller has nine more listings ready to assign if the first is strong, so consistency and a reusable, personalizable system matter more than one-off art.

The agent is given the seller's raw source assets - a couple engagement portrait, a hand-drawn gold monogram crest, a guest/RSVP CSV, a screen recording of the template scrolling through all pages (regenerated to >=30s of usable scroll), and a short raw voiceover clip - and must produce the full listing package faithful to the real brief, using EVERY step within real connector capability and with each step execution-mode tagged: (a) the personalized stationery front-end - a Save-the-Date and a Wedding-Details card built on the Express template track (search_design returns templates headless [C], then the user picks one in the gallery and the couple-name/date/venue placeholder TEXT is filled via fill_text [W], text-only, layout preserved), PLUS an authored RSVP MASTER built as a genuine connector-renderable VECTOR template (document_render_vector [C]) that carries real Illustrator Variables and the gold monogram composited as a placed image element, then data-merged (document_merge_data_vector [T]) against the 14-row guest CSV to emit exactly one personalized RSVP/guest card per row; (b) 8-10 Etsy listing mockup images rendered as burgundy phone-frame compositions (document_render_vector [C]) and then PIXEL-LOCKED to EXACTLY 2700x2025 px JPG/PNG via image_crop_and_resize [C], with feature badges (RSVP, Countdown Timer, Demo Link) and at least one close-up detail shot; (c) a 20-30s premium MP4 demo video produced by trimming the >=30s template-scroll screencap DOWN with video_create_quick_cut [A] (it shortens, never lengthens), reframed to 1080x1080 square via video_resize [A] (same length), delivered silent per the brief, plus an enriched narrated alternate that muxes a media_enhance_speech-cleaned [A] voiceover onto the square cut via a final local ffmpeg step [L]; and (d) a 1-2 page luxury PDF Buyer's Guide rendered straight from a genuine .indd/.idml layout (document_render_layout [C], no convert/merge misuse) explaining how to access, edit, and share the Canva template, matching the kit aesthetic (gold monogram, editorial portrait).

Hard constraints: keep the burgundy/cream/gold palette and the couple's names + wedding date identical across every artifact; mockups exactly 2700x2025 px (pixel-locked); demo video 20-30 seconds, MP4, premium product-preview quality with no required voiceover; buyer's guide 1-2 pages PDF in the matching luxury aesthetic; the merged RSVP set must contain exactly one personalized card per guest row (14). All TEXT personalization on the Express cards goes through the text-only fill path (layout preserved); image placement (the monogram) happens in a render step, never in fill_text; the data-merge runs against the authored vector template, not an Express design handle; the demo's length is set by quick_cut trimming a >=30s source, not by lengthening a short clip; and only the final audio/video mux falls back to local glue. The live Canva website with the working countdown timer and the editable share-link delivery remain the human's Canva handoff, out of scope for headless connector execution.

title: 2-Minute Arabic Algebra Explainer
desc:
Context: A client is commissioning a concise, two-minute educational video that teaches linear (one-variable) equations in clear, age-appropriate Arabic delivered with a genuine GCC (Gulf) accent. The piece is explicitly a MIXED format: short live-action segments featuring a boy aged 12–15, blended seamlessly with animated graphics that illustrate the algebra steps. The client will supply curriculum references; the freelancer handles scriptwriting, voice-over, casting/stock for the boy, animation, editing, and final mastering. The visual style must stay vibrant and engaging without distracting from the math — dynamic call-outs and simple motion graphics that walk a viewer through solving one basic linear equation and checking the answer.

The exact ask: deliver a finished explainer built on an Adobe Express animated lesson-frame template. Use the supplied reference lecture to derive the precise solving sequence (problem → isolate the variable → divide by the coefficient → state x → substitute and check). Produce four animated scenes (one per solve beat) carrying Arabic equation/step copy, interweave them IN A FIXED PEDAGOGICAL ORDER with the live-action boy footage into a single cut, lay the polished Arabic narration over the picture, and master to spec.

How the connector workflow realizes it (execution-mode honest): media_summarize [A] structures the reference lecture; the four scenes are TEXT-filled on a single user-picked Express template (search_design [C] returns the gallery, the user picks, fill_text [W] fills text only, animate_design [W] adds motion); media_enhance_speech [A] cleans the VOICE-only VO without altering the accent; video_resize [A] frame-locks every scene clip to 1920×1080 at the SAME length; and the ORDER-CRITICAL weave of the four scenes with the boy B-roll, the VO mux, the final exact-duration trim, AND the timecode RTL Arabic SRT are all done LOCALLY [L] with ffmpeg/alignment, because no connector tool sequences deterministically or emits time-aligned subtitles. The picture is pre-built to ~115–120s so only a light trim lands the exact 2:00.

Constraints / deliverables: (1) Final 1080p MP4 (1920×1080, H.264), runtime exactly 2:00 ±5 sec. (2) A SEPARATE Arabic SRT subtitle file (RTL, timed to the VO, built locally). (3) Editable source / scene artifacts for future edits (the four filled Express designs and the four frame-locked per-scene animation clips, bundled via asset_copy_assets). Acceptance criteria, all enforced: spoken Arabic matches a modern GCC accent throughout (and is NOT flattened by cleanup); live-action and animation are interwoven in a fixed order with NO abrupt cuts or style inconsistencies (style breaks are a reject); and by the end a learner can solve a one-variable linear equation AND check the answer.

title: Luxury Packaging Designer — Hyperrealistic Beauty Subscription Box
desc:
A new multi-brand beauty subscription service is launching and needs the physical packaging for its flagship box - the box that ships in every delivery and showcases several partner brands at once. The client's North Star is Gucci: clean, confident, impeccably finished, luxurious without trying too hard. They want a refined premium exterior with beautiful proportions and an impeccable finish; elevated soft-touch modern materials (they explicitly dislike embossing and hot-foil stamping - lean away from that look); a thoughtful interior structure whose inserts and dividers hold each brand's product securely and present them with room to breathe rather than crowded; considered details (a satisfying closure, a ribbon pull, a branded seal, a thoughtful card); and a premium, satisfying opening reveal. Restraint over ornamentation throughout. The client supplies their vision/reference imagery and expects the designer to elevate that direction into something original and distinctly their own.

The engagement is a collaborative two-phase process. Phase 1 (Concepts): at least three distinct design directions presented as concept boards/mockups, each a different take on structure, materials, finishes, interior arrangement, closure and opening, with a short walkthrough of the thinking. The client reviews, gives feedback, and approves one direction. Phase 2 (Final renders): once aligned, produce finished hyperrealistic imagery that reads as real product photography (not flat mockups) - exactly 3 hyperrealistic images of the box CLOSED and exactly 3 hyperrealistic images of the box OPEN showing the interior structure and how the multi-brand products are presented inside.

IMPORTANT CAPABILITY NOTE (v2.1 grounding): the supplied closed and open renders are generated ALREADY honoring the brand rules - the closed render ships with a flush matte foil-free seal medallion, and the open render ships with a breathable velvet divider cradling the multi-brand products. This is because the Adobe connector has NO generative-fill, NO AI object-removal, and NO AI object-replacement capability (only image_generative_expand outpaint is generative), so the banned-finish corrections cannot be done on-connector and are instead baked into the input generation. The connector's legitimate role is tonal normalization, color matching, geometry/crop, generative outpaint expansion, vectorization, board assembly, font recommendation, and video reframe/cut.

For this long-horizon engagement you are given a base hyperrealistic box render (closed, already clean), a base interior render (open, already breathable), a short horizontal reveal clip, a multi-brand product flat-lay, the client's vision-reference board, the partner-brand logos, and a brand palette spec. From these you must: normalize and color-lock the closed render and crop a canonical base; outpaint it into a wide closed-box hero (3000x1250) and produce a tighter detail crop so the closed set has exactly 3 frames; normalize/color-match/light the open render and outpaint it into a lifestyle unboxing scene (2400x2400) so the open set has exactly 3 frames; vectorize a structural dieline and rasterize a preview; assemble a single Firefly concept board for the Phase-1 review gate; recommend luxury typography; locally composite a 4:5 social teaser poster (2400x3000) and an A4/300dpi print-ready structure-and-finish PDF; and reframe the supplied reveal clip to vertical (1080x1920, same length) then quick-cut it into a ~8s vertical subscription teaser. Hard constraints: no embossing or hot-foil look anywhere; soft-touch matte finish; uncluttered, breathable interior; consistent box color across every closed-box and open-box derivative; deliver exactly 3 closed + 3 open hyperrealistic frames; wide hero 3000x1250, detail crop 1500x1500, lifestyle unboxing 2400x2400, poster 2400x3000 (4:5), print sheet A4 at 300dpi, teaser 1080x1920 ~8s.
